{\n97\t        Self::new()\n98\t    }\n99\t}\n100\t\n101\t// Custom serialization: only serialize workspaces, skip index (it's rebuilt)\n102\timpl Serialize for WorkspaceRegistry {\n103\t    fn std::result::Result<Self::Value, A::Error>\n127\t            where\n128\t                A: serde::de::SeqAccess<'de>,\n129\t            {\n130\t                let mut registry =       registry.workspaces.push(workspace);\n135\t                    registry.index.insert(id, registry.workspaces.len() - 1);\n136\t                }\n137\t\n138\t                Ok(registry)\n139\t  /// Add a workspace to the registry.\n156\t    pub fn add(&mut self, workspace: WorkspaceConfig) {\n157\t        let id = workspace.id.clone();\n158\t        self.workspaces.push(workspace);\n159\t        self.index.insert(id, self.workspaces.len() - 1);\n160\t    }\n161\t\n162\t    /// Remove a workspace by ID.\n163\t    pub fn remove(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<WorkspaceConfig> {\n164\t self.workspaces.remove(idx);\n168\t            // Then rebuild index with correct indices\n169\t            self.index.clear();\n170\t            for (i, ws) in self.workspaces.iter().enumerate() {\n171\t                self.index.insert(ws.id.clone(), i);\n172\t            }\n173\t            Some(workspace)\n174\t        } else {\n175\t            None\n176\t        }\n177\t    }\n178\t\n179\t    /// Get a workspace by ID.\n180\t    pub fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&WorkspaceConfig> {\n181\t        self.index.get(id).and_then(|&idx| self.workspaces.get(idx))\n182\t    }\n183\t\n184\t    /// Get a mutable reference to a workspace by ID.\n185\t    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut WorkspaceConfig> {\n186\t        if let Some(&idx) = self.all().into_iter().filter(|ws| ws.enabled).collect()\n203\t    }\n204\t\n205\t    /// Get the current/active workspace (first enabled by priority).\n206\t    pub fn current(&self) -> self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n212\t        if !self.index.contains_key(id) {\n213\t            return Err(ForgeError::WorkerNotFound {\n214\t                worker_id: format!(\"workspace {}\", id),\n215\t            });\n216\t        }\n217\t\n218\t        // Reorder by setting the current workspace to priority 0\n219\t        // and shifting others down\n220\t        if let Some(ws) = self.get_mut(id) {\n221\t            let old_priority = ws.priority;\n222\t            ws.priority = 0;\n223\t\n224\t            // Shift all other workspaces down\n225\t            for other_ws in &mut self.workspaces {\n226\t                if other_ws.id != id && other_ws.priority < old_priority {\n227\t                    other_ws.priority += 1;\n228\t                }\n229\t            }\n230\t        }\n231\t\n232\t        Ok(())\n233\t    }\n234\t\n235\t    /// Get workspace count.\n236\t    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {\n237\t        self.workspaces.len()\n238\t    }\n239\t\n240\t directory.\n246\t    pub fn discover_from_path(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {\n247\t        let mut registry = Self::new();\n248\t\n249\t        // If path is a workspace directory, add it\n250\t file.\n272\t    pub fn load_from_file(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {\n273\t        if !path.exists() {\n274\t            warn!(\"Workspace config file not found: {:?}\", path);\n275\t            return Ok(Self::new());\n276\t        }\n277\t\n278\t        let content = fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n279\t            operation: \"read\".to_string(),\n280\t            path: path.to_path_buf(),\n281\t            source: e,\n282\t        })?;\n283\t\n284\t        let mut registry: Self = serde_yaml::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| {\n285\t            ForgeError::YamlParse {\n286\t                context: format!(\"workspace config at {:?}\", path),\n287\t                message: e.to_string(),\n288\t            }\n289\t        })?;\n290\t\n291\t    /// Save workspace registry to a YAML configuration file.\n299\t    pub fn save_to_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n300\t        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n301\t            fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n302\t                operation: \"create directory\".to_string(),\n303\t                path: parent.to_path_buf(),\n304\t                source: e,\n305\t            })?;\n306\t        }\n307\t\n308\t        let yaml = serde_yaml::to_string(self).map_err(|e| ForgeError::YamlParse {\n309\t            context: \"workspace registry path);\n320\t        Ok(())\n321\t    }\n322\t\n323\t    /// Rebuild the index after deserialization or manual modification.\n324\t    fn rebuild_index(&mut self) {\n325\t        self.index.clear();\n326\t        for (i, ws) in self.workspaces.iter().enumerate() {\n327\t            self.index.insert(ws.id.clone(), i);\n328\t        }\n329\t    }\n330\t\n331\t    /// Validate all workspaces and report issues.\n332\t    pub fn validate(&self) -> Vec<WorkspaceIssue> {\n333\t        let mut issues = Vec::new();\n334\t\n335\t        for ws in &self.workspaces {\n336\t            if !ws.enabled {\n337\t                continue;\n338\t            }\n339\t\n340\t            if !ws.is_accessible() {\n341\t                issues.push(WorkspaceIssue {\n342\t pub fn aggregate_stats(&self) -> WorkspaceAggregateStats {\n372\t        let mut stats = WorkspaceAggregateStats::default();\n373\t\n374\t        for ws in &self.workspaces {\n375\t            if !ws.enabled || !ws.is_accessible() {\n376\t                continue;\n377\t            }\n378\t\n379\t            stats.total_workspaces += 1;\n380\t\n381\t            if ws.has_cost_data() {\n382\t {\n443\t    /// All beads across workspaces\n444\t    pub beads: Vec<WorkspaceBead>,\n445\t    /// Beads grouped by workspace\n446\t    pub by_workspace: HashMap<String, Vec<WorkspaceBead>>,\n447\t    /// Total open beads\n448\t    pub total_open: usize,\n449\t    /// Total closed beads\n450\t    pub total_closed: usize,\n451\t    /// Unassigned beads\n452\t    pub unassigned: usize,\n453\t}\n454\t\n455\timpl CrossWorkspaceBeadResult {\n456\t    /// Get beads for a specific workspace.\n457\t    pub fn beads_for_workspace(&self, workspace_id: &str) -> Vec<&WorkspaceBead> {\n458\t        self.by_workspace\n459\t            .get(workspace_id)\n460\t            .map(|v| v.iter().collect())\n461\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n462\t    }\n463\t\n464\t    /// Get open beads across all workspaces.\n465\t    pub fn open_beads(&self) -> Vec<&WorkspaceBead> {\n466\t        self.beads\n467\t            .iter()\n468\t            .filter(|b| b.status == \"open\" || b.status == \"in-progress\")\n469\t            .collect()\n470\t    }\n471\t\n472\t    /// Get unassigned beads.\n473\t    pub fn unassigned_beads(&self) -> Vec<&WorkspaceBead> {\n474\t        self.beads\n475\t            .iter()\n476\t            .filter(|b| b.assignee.is_none() || b.assignee.as_deref() == Some(\"none\"))\n477\t            .collect()\n478\t    }\n479\t}\n480\t\n481\t/// Query beads across multiple workspaces.\n482\t///\n483\t/// This function reads beads JSONL files from each workspace's .beads directory\n484\t/// and aggregates them into a unified view.\n485\t///\n486\t/// # Example\n487\t///\n488\t/// ```no_run\n489\t/// use forge_core::{WorkspaceRegistry, query_beads_cross_workspace};\n490\t///\n491\t/// fn main() -> forge_core::Result<()> {\n492\t///     let registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n493\t///     let result = query_beads_cross_workspace(&registry)?;\n494\t///\n495\t///     println!(\"Found {} beads across {} workspaces\",\n496\t///         result.beads.len(),\n497\t///         result.by_workspace.len()\n498\t///     );\n499\t///\n500\t///     Ok(())\n501\t/// }\n502\t/// ```\n503\tpub fn query_beads_cross_workspace(\n504\t    registry: &WorkspaceRegistry,\n505\t) -> Result<CrossWorkspaceBeadResult> {\n506\t    let mut result = CrossWorkspaceBeadResult::default();\n507\t\n508\t    for ws in registry.all() {\n509\t        let beads_dir = ws.beads_dir();\n510\t\n511\t        if !beads_dir.exists() {\n512\t            continue;\n513\t        }\n514\t\n515\t        let issues_path = beads_dir.join(\"issues.jsonl\");\n516\t\n517\t        if !issues_path.exists() {\n518\t            continue;\n519\t        }\n520\t\n521\t        // Read and parse beads JSONL\n522\t        match read_beads_from_jsonl(&issues_path, &ws.id, &ws.name) {\n523\t            Ok(mut beads) => {\n524\t                result.total_open += beads.iter().filter(|b| b.status == \"open\" || b.status == \"in-progress\").count();\n525\t                result.total_closed += beads.iter().filter(|b| b.status == \"closed\").count();\n526\t                result.unassigned += beads.iter().filter(|b| b.assignee.is_none() || b.assignee.as_deref() == Some(\"none\")).count();\n527\t\n528\t                result.by_workspace.insert(ws.id.clone(), beads.clone());\n529\t                result.beads.append(&mut beads);\n530\t            }\n531\t            Err(e) => {\n532\t                tracing::warn!(\n533\t                    \"Failed to read beads from workspace {}: {}\",\n534\t                    ws.id,\n535\t                    e\n536\t                );\n537\t            }\n538\t        }\n539\t    }\n540\t\n541\t    tracing::debug!(\n542\t        \"Queried {} beads across {} workspaces ({} open, {} closed)\",\n543\t        result.beads.len(),\n544\t        result.by_workspace.len(),\n545\t        result.total_open,\n546\t        result.total_closed\n547\t    );\n548\t\n549\t    Ok(result)\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t/// Read beads from a JSONL file.\n553\tfn read_beads_from_jsonl(\n554\t    path: &Path,\n555\t    workspace_id: &str,\n556\t    workspace_name: &str,\n557\t) -> Result<Vec<WorkspaceBead>> {\n558\t    let content = fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n559\t        operation: \"read\".to_string(),\n560\t        path: path.to_path_buf(),\n561\t        source: e,\n562\t    })?;\n563\t\n564\t    let mut beads = Vec::new();\n565\t\n566\t    for line in content.lines() {\n567\t        if line.trim().is_empty() {\n568\t            continue;\n569\t        }\n570\t\n571\t        // Parse JSONL line\n572\t        if let Ok(value) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line) {\n573\t            let id = value.get(\"id\")\n574\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n575\t                .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n576\t                .to_string();\n577\t\n578\t            let title = value.get(\"title\")\n579\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n580\t                .map(|s| s.to_string());\n581\t\n582\t            let status = value.get(\"status\")\n583\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n584\t                .unwrap_or(\"open\")\n585\t                .to_string();\n586\t\n587\t            let assignee = value.get(\"assignee\")\n588\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n589\t                .filter(|s| !s.is_empty() && *s != \"none\")\n590\t                .map(|s| s.to_string());\n591\t\n592\t            let priority = value.get(\"priority\")\n593\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n594\t                .map(|s| s.to_string());\n595\t\n596\t            beads.push(WorkspaceBead {\n597\t                id,\n598\t                workspace_id: workspace_id.to_string(),\n599\t                workspace_name: workspace_name.to_string(),\n600\t                title,\n601\t                status,\n602\t                assignee,\n603\t                priority,\n604\t            });\n605\t        }\n606\t    }\n607\t\n608\t    Ok(beads)\n609\t}\n610\t\n611\t/// Get bead count for a specific workspace.\n612\tpub fn get_workspace_bead_count(workspace_path: &Path) -> Result<usize> {\n613\t    let beads_dir = workspace_path.join(\".beads\");\n614\t    let issues_path = beads_dir.join(\"issues.jsonl\");\n615\t\n616\t    if !issues_path.exists() {\n617\t        return Ok(0);\n618\t    }\n619\t\n620\t    let content = fs::read_to_string(&issues_path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n621\t        operation: \"read\".to_string(),\n622\t        path: issues_path.clone(),\n623\t        source: e,\n624\t    })?;\n625\t\n626\t    let count = content.lines().filter(|l| !l.trim().is_empty()).count();\n627\t\n628\t    Ok(count)\n629\t}\n630\t\n631\t/// Get bead counts for all workspaces in a registry.\n632\tpub fn get_workspace_bead_counts(\n633\t    registry: &WorkspaceRegistry,\n634\t) -> Result<HashMap<String, usize>> {\n635\t    let mut counts = HashMap::new();\n636\t\n637\t    for ws in registry.all() {\n638\t        if !ws.is_accessible() {\n639\t            continue;\n640\t        }\n641\t\n642\t        match get_workspace_bead_count(&ws.path) {\n643\t            Ok(count) => {\n644\t                counts.insert(ws.id.clone(), count);\n645\t            }\n646\t            Err(e) => {\n647\t                tracing::warn!(\n648\t                    \"Failed to get bead count for workspace {}: {}\",\n649\t                    ws.id,\n650\t                    e\n651\t                );\n652\t            }\n653\t        }\n654\t    }\n655\t\n656\t    Ok(counts)\n657\t}\n658\t\n659\t#[cfg(test)]\n660\tmod tests {\n661\t    use super::*;\n662\t }\n71\t}\n72\t\n73\t/// Aggregator for costs across multiple workspaces.\n74\tpub struct MultiWorkspaceCostAggregator {\n75\t    /// Path to each workspace's cost database\n76\t    workspace_dbs: /// Aggregate costs for a specific date across all workspaces.\n123\t    pub fn aggregate_costs_for_date(&self, date: NaiveDate) -> Result<MultiWorkspaceCostSummary> {\n124\t        let mut summary = summary.by_workspace.insert(workspace_id.clone(), ws_cost);\n150\t                }\n151\t                Ok(None) => {\n152\t                    // Workspace has no cost data for this date\n153\t .to_path_buf(),\n164\t                            has_cost_data: false,\n165\t                            today_cost: None,\n166\t                            total_cost: 0.0,\n167\t                            worker_count: 0,\n168\t                        },\n169\t                    );\n170\t                }\n171\t                Err(e) => {\n172\t                    warn!(\"Failed to query cost   Ok(summary)\n185\t    }\n186\t\n187\t    /// Aggregate costs across all workspaces for a date range.\n188\t    pub fn aggregate_costs_range(\n189\t        &self,\n190\t        start_date:      warn!(\"Failed to query cost range for workspace {}: {}\", workspace_id, e);\n220\t                    summary.workspaces_without_costs += 1;\n221\t                }\n222\t            }\n223\t        }\n224\t\n225\t        Ok(summary)\n226\t    }\n227\t\n228\t    /// Query cost for a specific workspace on a specific date.\n229\t    fn query_workspace_cost(\n230\t        &self,\n231\t  Ok(Some(daily))\n241\t                } else {\n242\t                    Ok(None)\n243\t                }\n244\t            }\n245\t            Err(e) => {\n246\t                // Treat query errors }\n252\t\n253\t    /// Query cost for a specific workspace over a date range.\n254\t    fn query_workspace_cost_range(\n255\t        &self,\n256\t        db_path: &Path,\n257\t        start_date: NaiveDate,\n258\t        end_date: NaiveDate,\n259\t    ) -> Result<f64> {\n260\t        let db = CostDatabase::open(db_path)?;\n261\t        let conn = db.connection();\n262\t        let conn = start_date.format(\"%Y-%m-%d\").to_string();\n267\t        let end = end_date.format(\"%Y-%m-%d\").to_string();\n268\t\n269\t        let total: f64 = conn\n270\t            .query_row(\n271\t }\n304\t        }\n305\t\n306\t        Ok(model_costs)\n307\t    }\n308\t\n309\t    /// Get cost breakdown by workspace for today.\n310\t    pub fn get_workspace_breakdown_today(&self) ->  location.column(),\n24\t            error\n25\t        )\n26\t    } else {\n27\t        format!(\"YAML parse error: {}\", error)\n28\t    }\n29\t}\n30\t\n31\t/// Errors that can occur when loading configuration.\n32\t#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n33\tpub enum ConfigLoadError {\n34\t    /// Could not determine home directory\n35\t    #[error(\"Could not determine home directory\")]\n36\t    NoHomePath,\n37\t\n38\t    /// Config file not found\n39\t    #[error(\"Config file not found: {0}\")]\n40\t    NotFound(PathBuf),\n41\t\n42\t    /// Error reading config file\n43\t    #[error(\"Failed to read config file {path}: {error}\")]\n44\t    ReadError {\n45\t        path: PathBuf,\n46\t        #[source]\n47\t        error: io::Error,\n48\t    },\n49\t\n50\t    /// Error  },\n56\t\n57\t    /// Config validation failed\n58\t    #[error(\"Config validation failed: {0}\")]\n59\t    ValidationError(String),\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\timpl From<String> for ConfigLoadError {\n63\t error.location().map(|loc| loc.line())\n74\t            }\n75\t            _ => None,\n76\t        }\n77\t    }\n78\t\n79\t    /// Get the column number where the error occurred (if available).\n80\t    pub fn column_number(&self) -> Option<usize> {\n81\t        match self {\n82\t            ConfigLoadError::ParseError { error, .. } => {\n83\t                error.location().map(|loc| pub workspaces: MultiWorkspaceConfig,\n163\t}\n164\t\n165\timpl ForgeConfig {\n166\t    /// Load configuration from the default path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\n167\t    ///\n168\t    /// Returns default configuration if the file doesn't exist or is invalid.\n169\t    /// Invalid configs are logged as warnings but don't prevent startup.\n170\t    pub fn load() -> Option<Self> {\n171\t        let path = config_path()?;\n172\t        Self::load_from(&path)\n173\t    }\n174\t\n175\t    /// Load configuration from the default path with detailed error reporting.\n176\t    ///\n177\t    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n178\t    pub fn load_with_error() -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n179\t        let path = config_path().ok_or(ConfigLoadError::NoHomePath)?;\n180\t        Self::load_from_with_error(&path)\n181\t    }\n182\t\n183\t    /// Load configuration from a specific path with graceful fallback.\n184\t    ///\n185\t    /// This method attempts to load and parse the config file. If the file\n186\t    /// doesn't exist, is unreadable, or contains invalid YAML, it returns\n187\t    /// the default configuration rather than failing.\n188\t    ///\n189\t    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent the application from starting.\n190\t    pub fn load_from(path: &PathBuf) -> Option<Self> {\n191\t        if !path.exists() {\n192\t            tracing::debug!(\"Config file does not exist: {:?} - using defaults\", path);\n193\t            return None;\n194\t        }\n195\t\n196\t        // Try to read the file\n197\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n198\t            Ok(c) => c,\n199\t            Err(e) => {\n200\t                tracing::warn!(\n201\t                    path = ?path,\n202\t                    error = %e,\n203\t                    \"Failed to read config file - using defaults\"\n204\t                );\n205\t                return None;\n206\t            }\n207\t        };\n208\t\n209\t        // Try to parse with fallback to partial parsing\n210\t        Self::parse_with_fallback(&content)\n211\t    }\n212\t\n213\t    /// Load configuration from a specific path with detailed error reporting.\n214\t    ///\n215\t    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n216\t    pub fn load_from_with_error(path: &PathBuf) -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n217\t        if !path.exists() {\n218\t            return Err(ConfigLoadError::NotFound(path.clone()));\n219\t        }\n220\t\n221\t        // Try to read the file\n222\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n223\t            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ReadError {\n224\t                path: path.clone(),\n225\t                error: e,\n226\t            })?;\n227\t\n228\t        // Try to parse as full YAML\n229\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(&content) {\n230\t            Ok(config) => {\n231\t                // Validate and return errors if invalid\n232\t                config.validate()?;\n233\t                Ok(config)\n234\t            }\n235\t            Err(e) => Err(ConfigLoadError::ParseError {\n236\t                path: path.clone(),\n237\t                error: e,\n238\t            }),\n239\t        }\n240\t    }\n241\t\n242\t    /// Parse configuration from YAML string.\n243\t    ///\n244\t    /// Returns None if parsing fails completely.\n245\t    pub fn parse(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n246\t        Self::parse_with_fallback(content)\n247\t    }\n248\t\n249\t    /// Parse configuration with fallback for partial/invalid configs.\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// This method:\n252\t    /// 1. Tries to parse the full config\n253\t    /// 2. Falls back to partial parsing if sections are invalid\n254\t    /// 3. Returns default for completely invalid YAML\n255\t    fn parse_with_fallback(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n256\t        // First try to parse as full YAML\n257\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(content) {\n258\t            Ok(config) => {\n259\t                // Validate and warn about issues, but still return the config\n260\t                if let Err(e) = config.validate() {\n261\t                    tracing::warn!(\n262\t                        error = %e,\n263\t                        \"Config validation warning - some settings may be ignored\"\n264\t                    );\n265\t                }\n266\t                tracing::debug!(\"Successfully parsed forge config\");\n267\t                Some(config)\n268\t            }\n269\t            Err(e) => {\n270\t                // Format detailed error message with line/column information\n271\t                let error_msg = format_yaml_error(&e);\n272\t                tracing::warn!(\n273\t                    error = %error_msg,\n274\t                    \"Failed to parse config YAML - attempting partial parse\"\n275\t                );\n276\t\n277\t                // Try to parse individual sections as a fallback\n278\t                Self::parse_partial(content)\n279\t            }\n280\t        }\n281\t    }\n282\t\n283\t    /// Attempt to parse individual sections of a malformed config.\n284\t    ///\n285\t    /// This allows partial configs to work even if one section has errors.\n286\t    fn parse_partial(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n287\t        // Try to parse as generic YAML first\n288\t        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(content) {\n289\t            Ok(y) => y,\n290\t            Err(e) => {\n291\t                tracing::warn!(\n292\t                    error = %e,\n293\t                    \"Config is not valid YAML - using defaults\"\n294\t                );\n295\t                return None;\n296\t            }\n297\t        };\n298\t\n299\t        // Try to extract individual sections\n300\t        let dashboard = yaml\n301\t            .get(\"dashboard\")\n302\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n303\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n304\t\n305\t        let theme = yaml\n306\t            .get(\"theme\")\n307\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n308\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n309\t\n310\t        let cost_tracking = yaml\n311\t            .get(\"cost_tracking\")\n312\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n313\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n314\t\n315\t        let auto_recovery = yaml\n316\t            .get(\"auto_recovery\")\n317\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n318\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n319\t\n320\t        let workers = yaml\n321\t            .get(\"workers\")\n322\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n323\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n324\t\n325\t        let notifications = yaml\n326\t            .get(\"notifications\")\n327\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n328\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n329\t\n330\t        let workspaces = yaml\n331\t            .get(\"workspaces\")\n332\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n333\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n334\t\n335\t        tracing::info!(\"Loaded partial config - some sections may use defaults\");\n336\t\n337\t        Some(Self {\n338\t            dashboard,\n339\t            theme,\n340\t            cost_tracking,\n341\t            auto_recovery,\n342\t            workers,\n343\t            notifications,\n344\t            workspaces,\n345\t        })\n346\t    }\n347\t\n348\t    /// Validate the configuration.\n349\t    ///\n350\t    /// Returns Ok(()) if valid, Err with message if invalid.\n351\t    /// Warnings are logged but don't cause validation failure.\n352\t    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n353\t        let mut warnings = Vec::new();\n354\t\n355\t        // Validate refresh This module provides utilities for discovering active worker sessions from tmux,\n4\t//! parsing session names to extract worker types, and returning structured data\n5\t//! about active and idle workers.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Session Naming Convention\n8\t//!\n9\t//! Worker sessions follow the pattern: `<executor>-<suffix>`\n10\t//!\n11\t//! Where executor patterns are:\n12\t//! - `claude-code-glm-47` → GLM-4.7 model via z.ai proxy\n13\t//! - `claude-code-sonnet` → Claude Sonnet 4.5\n14\t//! - `claude-code-opus` → Claude Opus 4.5\n15\t//! - `claude-code-haiku` → Claude Haiku 4.5\n16\t//! - `opencode-glm-47` → OpenCode with GLM-4.7\n17\t//!\n18\t//! # Example\n19\t//!\n20\t//! ```no_run\n21\t//! use forge_worker::discovery::{discover_workers, WorkerType};\n22\t//!\n23\t//! #[tokio::main]\n24\t//! async fn main() -> forge_core::Result<()> {\n25\t//!     let result = discover_workers().await?;\n26\t//!\n27\t//!     for worker in &result.workers {\n28\t//!         println!(\"{}: {} ({})\",\n29\t//!             worker.session_name,\n30\t//!             worker.worker_type,\n31\t//!             if worker.is_attached { \"attached\" } else { \"detached\" }\n32\t//!         );\n33\t//!     }\n34\t//!\n35\t//!     Ok(())\n36\t//! }\n37\t//! ```\n38\t\n39\tuse chrono::{DateTime, TimeZone, Utc};\n40\tuse forge_core::{ForgeError, Result, WorkspaceRegistry};\n41\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n42\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n43\tuse std::fmt;\n44\tuse std::path::Path;\n45\tuse tokio::process::Command;\n46\tuse tracing::{debug, instrument, warn};\n47\t\n48\t/// Worker type based on the model/executor being used.\n49\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n50\t#[serde(rename_all = \"kebab-case\")]\n51\tpub enum WorkerType {\n52\t    /// GLM-4.7 model via z.ai proxy (Claude Code or OpenCode)\n53\t    Glm47,\n54\t    /// Claude Sonnet 4.5\n55\t    Sonnet,\n56\t    /// Claude Opus 4.5/4.6\n57\t    Opus,\n58\t    /// Claude Haiku 4.5\n59\t    Haiku,\n60\t    /// Unknown/unrecognized worker type\n61\t    Unknown,\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\timpl WorkerType {\n65\t    /// Parse a worker type from a session name.\n66\t    ///\n67\t    /// Matches against known executor patterns:\n68\t    /// - `claude-code-glm-47-*` or `opencode-glm-47-*` → Glm47\n69\t    /// - `claude-code-sonnet-*` → Sonnet\n70\t    /// - `claude-code-opus-*` → Opus\n71\t    /// - `claude-code-haiku-*` → Haiku\n72\t    pub fn from_session_name(name: &str) -> Self {\n73\t        if name.contains(\"glm-47\") || name.contains(\"glm47\") {\n74\t            Self::Glm47\n75\t        } else if name.contains(\"sonnet\") {\n76\t            Self::Sonnet\n77\t        } else if name.contains(\"opus\") {\n78\t            Self::Opus\n79\t        } else if name.contains(\"haiku\") {\n80\t            Self::Haiku\n81\t        } else {\n82\t            Self::Unknown\n83\t        }\n84\t    }\n85\t\n86\t    /// Get a short display name for the worker type.\n87\t    pub fn short_name(&self) -> &'static str {\n88\t        match self {\n89\t            Self::Glm47 => \"GLM-4.7\",\n90\t            Self::Sonnet => \"Sonnet\",\n91\t            Self::Opus => \"Opus\",\n92\t            Self::Haiku => \"Haiku\",\n93\t            Self::Unknown => \"Unknown\",\n94\t        }\n95\t    }\n96\t\n97\t    /// Get the approximate cost tier (for display purposes).\n98\t    pub fn cost_tier(&self) -> &'static str {\n99\t        match self {\n100\t            Self::Opus => \"high\",\n101\t            Self::Sonnet => \"medium\",\n102\t            Self::Haiku | Self::Glm47 => \"low\",\n103\t            Self::Unknown => \"unknown\",\n104\t        }\n105\t    }\n106\t}\n107\t\n108\timpl fmt::Display for WorkerType {\n109\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n110\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.short_name())\n111\t    }\n112\t}\n113\t\n114\t/// Information about a discovered worker session.\n115\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n116\tpub struct DiscoveredWorker {\n117\t    /// The tmux session name\n118\t    pub session_name: String,\n119\t    /// Parsed worker type from the session name\n120\t    pub worker_type: WorkerType,\n121\t    /// Unix timestamp when the session was created\n122\t    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n123\t    /// Unix timestamp of last activity in the session\n124\t    pub last_activity: DateTime<Utc>,\n125\t    /// Whether a client is currently attached to this session\n126\t    pub is_attached: bool,\n127\t    /// The worker suffix (e.g., \"alpha\", \"bravo\", \"charlie\")\n128\t    pub suffix: String,\n129\t    /// The executor prefix (e.g., \"claude-code-glm-47\", \"opencode-glm-47\")\n130\t    pub executor: String,\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\timpl DiscoveredWorker {\n134\t    /// Check if this worker appears to be idle based on activity time.\n135\t    ///\n136\t    /// A worker is considered idle if there has been no activity for the given duration.\n137\t    pub fn is_idle(&self, idle_threshold_secs: i64) -> bool {\n138\t        let now = Utc::now();\n139\t        let inactive_secs = (now - self.last_activity).num_seconds();\n140\t        inactive_secs > idle_threshold_secs\n141\t    }\n142\t\n143\t    /// Get the human-readable session age.\n144\t    pub fn age(&self) -> String {\n145\t        let now = Utc::now();\n146\t        let duration = now - self.created_at;\n147\t\n148\t        if duration.num_days() > 0 {\n149\t            format!(\"{}d\", duration.num_days())\n150\t        } else if duration.num_hours() > 0 {\n151\t            format!(\"{}h\", duration.num_hours())\n152\t        } else if duration.num_minutes() > 0 {\n153\t            format!(\"{}m\", duration.num_minutes())\n154\t        } else {\n155\t            format!(\"{}s\", duration.num_seconds())\n156\t        }\n157\t    }\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\t/// Result of a worker discovery operation.\n161\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n162\tpub struct DiscoveryResult {\n163\t    /// All discovered worker sessions\n164\t    pub workers: Vec<DiscoveredWorker>,\n165\t    /// Count by worker type\n166\t    pub by_type: std::collections::HashMap<WorkerType, usize>,\n167\t    /// Total attached (active) sessions\n168\t    pub attached_count: usize,\n169\t    /// Total detached (potentially idle) sessions\n170\t    pub detached_count: usize,\n171\t}\n172\t\n173\timpl DiscoveryResult {\n174\t    /// Get workers of a specific type.\n175\t    pub fn workers_of_type(&self, worker_type: WorkerType) -> Vec<&DiscoveredWorker> {\n176\t        self.workers\n177\t            .iter()\n178\t            .filter(|w| w.worker_type == worker_type)\n179\t            .collect()\n180\t    }\n181\t\n182\t    /// Get attached (active) workers.\n183\t    pub fn attached_workers(&self) -> Vec<&DiscoveredWorker> {\n184\t        self.workers.iter().filter(|w| w.is_attached).collect()\n185\t    }\n186\t\n187\t    /// Get detached workers.\n188\t    pub fn detached_workers(&self) -> Vec<&DiscoveredWorker> {\n189\t        self.workers.iter().filter(|w| !w.is_attached).collect()\n190\t    }\n191\t\n192\t    /// Get idle workers based on activity threshold.\n193\t    pub fn idle_workers(&self, idle_threshold_secs: i64) -> Vec<&DiscoveredWorker> {\n194\t        self.workers\n195\t            .iter()\n196\t            .filter(|w| w.is_idle(idle_threshold_secs))\n197\t            .collect()\n198\t    }\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\t/// Known worker session prefixes for filtering.\n202\tconst WORKER_PREFIXES: &[&str] = &[\n203\t    \"claude-code-glm-47-\",\n204\t    \"claude-code-sonnet-\",\n205\t    \"claude-code-opus-\",\n206\t    \"claude-code-haiku-\",\n207\t    \"opencode-glm-47-\",\n208\t];\n209\t\n210\t/// Parse a session line from tmux list-sessions output.\n211\t///\n212\t/// Expected format from: `tmux list-sessions -F '#{session_name}:#{session_created}:#{session_activity}:#{session_attached}'`\n213\t/// Example: `claude-code-glm-47-alpha:1770587037:1770587037:0`\n214\tfn parse_session_line(line: &str) -> Option<DiscoveredWorker> {\n215\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = line.split(':').collect();\n216\t    if parts.len() < 4 {\n217\t        warn!(\"Invalid session line format: {}\", line);\n218\t        return None;\n219\t    }\n220\t\n221\t    let session_name = parts[0].to_string();\n222\t\n223\t    // Filter to only known worker sessions\n224\t    let is_worker = WORKER_PREFIXES.iter().any(|p| session_name.starts_with(p));\n225\t    if !is_worker {\n226\t        return None;\n227\t    }\n228\t\n229\t    // Parse timestamps\n230\t    let created_ts: i64 = parts[1].parse().ok()?;\n231\t    let activity_ts: i64 = parts[2].parse().ok()?;\n232\t    let attached: i32 = parts[3].parse().ok()?;\n233\t\n234\t    let created_at = Utc.timestamp_opt(created_ts, 0).single()?;\n235\t    let last_activity = Utc.timestamp_opt(activity_ts, 0).single()?;\n236\t\n237\t    // Extract executor and suffix\n238\t    let (executor, suffix) = extract_executor_and_suffix(&session_name)?;\n239\t    let worker_type = WorkerType::from_session_name(&session_name);\n240\t\n241\t    Some(DiscoveredWorker {\n242\t        session_name,\n243\t        worker_type,\n244\t        created_at,\n245\t        last_activity,\n246\t        is_attached: attached > 0,\n247\t        suffix,\n248\t        executor,\n249\t    })\n250\t}\n251\t\n252\t/// Extract executor prefix and suffix from a session name.\n253\t///\n254\t/// Example: \"claude-code-glm-47-alpha\" → (\"claude-code-glm-47\", \"alpha\")\n255\tfn extract_executor_and_suffix(session_name: &str) -> Option<(String, String)> {\n256\t    for prefix in WORKER_PREFIXES {\n257\t        if session_name.starts_with(prefix) {\n258\t            let suffix = session_name.strip_prefix(prefix)?;\n259\t            // Remove trailing hyphen from prefix for clean executor name\n260\t            let executor = prefix.trim_end_matches('-').to_string();\n261\t            return Some((executor, suffix.to_string()));\n262\t        }\n263\t    }\n264\t    None\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\t/// Discover all active worker sessions from tmux.\n268\t///\n269\t/// This queries `tmux list-sessions` and parses the output to find\n270\t/// worker sessions matching known patterns.\n271\t#[instrument(level = \"debug\")]\n272\tpub async fn discover_workers() -> Result<DiscoveryResult> {\n273\t    let output = Command::new(\"tmux\")\n274\t        .args([\n275\t            \"list-sessions\",\n276\t            \"-F\",\n277\t            \"#{session_name}:#{session_created}:#{session_activity}:#{session_attached}\",\n278\t        ])\n279\t        .output()\n280\t        .await\n281\t        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::WorkerSpawn {\n282\t            worker_id: \"discovery\".into(),\n283\t            message: format!(\"Failed to list tmux sessions: {}\", e),\n284\t        })?;\n285\t\n286\t    if !output.status.success() {\n287\t        // No tmux server or no sessions is not an error\n288\t        let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);\n289\t        if stderr.contains(\"no server running\") || stderr.contains(\"no sessions\") {\n290\t            debug!(\"No tmux sessions found\");\n291\t            return Ok(DiscoveryResult::default());\n292\t        }\n293\t\n294\t        return Err(ForgeError::WorkerSpawn {\n295\t            worker_id: \"discovery\".into(),\n296\t            message: format!(\"tmux list-sessions failed: {}\", stderr.trim()),\n297\t        });\n298\t    }\n299\t\n300\t    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\n301\t    let workers: Vec<DiscoveredWorker> = stdout.lines().filter_map(parse_session_line).collect();\n302\t\n303\t    // Build statistics\n304\t    let mut by_type = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n305\t    let mut attached_count = 0;\n306\t    let mut detached_count = 0;\n307\t\n308\t    for worker in &workers {\n309\t        *by_type.entry(worker.worker_type).or_insert(0) += 1;\n310\t        if worker.is_attached {\n311\t            attached_count += 1;\n312\t        } else {\n313\t            detached_count += 1;\n314\t        }\n315\t    }\n316\t\n317\t    debug!(\n318\t        \"Discovered {} worker sessions ({} attached, {} detached)\",\n319\t        workers.len(),\n320\t        attached_count,\n321\t        detached_count\n322\t    );\n323\t\n324\t    Ok(DiscoveryResult {\n325\t        workers,\n326\t        by_type,\n327\t        attached_count,\n328\t        detached_count,\n329\t    })\n330\t}\n331\t\n332\t/// Discover workers of a specific type.\n333\t#[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip_all, fields(worker_type = %worker_type))]\n334\tpub async fn discover_workers_by_type(worker_type: WorkerType) -> Result<Vec<DiscoveredWorker>> {\n335\t    let result = discover_workers().await?;\n336\t    Ok(result\n337\t        .workers\n338\t        .into_iter()\n339\t        .filter(|w| w.worker_type == worker_type)\n340\t        .collect())\n341\t}\n342\t\n343\t/// Get a summary of discovered workers for display.\n344\tpub async fn discovery_summary() -> Result<String> {\n345\t    let result = discover_workers().await?;\n346\t\n347\t    if result.workers.is_empty() {\n348\t        return Ok(\"No worker sessions found\".to_string());\n349\t    }\n350\t\n351\t    let mut summary = format!(\n352\t        \"Workers: {} total ({} attached, {} detached)\\n\",\n353\t        result.workers.len(),\n354\t        result.attached_count,\n355\t        result.detached_count\n356\t    );\n357\t\n358\t    for (worker_type, count) in &result.by_type {\n359\t        summary.push_str(&format!(\"  {}: {}\\n\", worker_type, count));\n360\t    }\n361\t\n362\t    Ok(summary)\n363\t}\n364\t\n365\t// ============================================================\n366\t// Cross-Workspace Discovery\n367\t// ============================================================\n368\t\n369\t/// Worker information with associated workspace context.\n370\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n371\tpub struct WorkspaceWorker {\n372\t    /// The discovered worker\n373\t    pub worker: DiscoveredWorker,\n374\t    /// Workspace ID this worker belongs to\n375\t    pub workspace_id: String,\n376\t    /// Workspace name\n377\t    pub workspace_name: String,\n378\t    /// Workspace path\n379\t    pub workspace_path: String,\n380\t}\n381\t\n382\t/// Result of cross-workspace worker discovery.\n383\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n384\tpub struct CrossWorkspaceDiscoveryResult {\n385\t    /// All workers across workspaces\n386\t    pub workers: Vec<WorkspaceWorker>,\n387\t    /// Workers grouped by workspace\n388\t    pub by_workspace: HashMap<String, Vec<WorkspaceWorker>>,\n389\t    /// Count by worker type across all workspaces\n390\t    pub by_type: HashMap<WorkerType, usize>,\n391\t    /// Total attached workers across all workspaces\n392\t    pub total_attached: usize,\n393\t    /// Total detached workers across all workspaces\n394\t    pub total_detached: usize,\n395\t}\n396\t\n397\timpl CrossWorkspaceDiscoveryResult {\n398\t    /// Get workers for a specific workspace.\n399\t    pub fn workers_for_workspace(&self, workspace_id: &str) -> Vec<&WorkspaceWorker> {\n400\t        self.by_workspace\n401\t            .get(workspace_id)\n402\t            .map(|v| v.iter().collect())\n403\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n404\t    }\n405\t\n406\t    /// Get workers of a specific type across all workspaces.\n407\t    pub fn workers_of_type(&self, worker_type: WorkerType) -> Vec<&WorkspaceWorker> {\n408\t        self.workers\n409\t            .iter()\n410\t            .filter(|w| w.worker.worker_type == worker_type)\n411\t            .collect()\n412\t    }\n413\t\n414\t    /// Get workspace with the most workers.\n415\t    pub fn most_active_workspace(&self) -> Option<&str> {\n416\t        self.by_workspace\n417\t            .iter()\n418\t            .max_by_key(|(_, workers)| workers.len())\n419\t            .map(|(id, _)| id.as_str())\n420\t    }\n421\t}\n422\t\n423\t/// Discover workers across multiple workspaces.\n424\t///\n425\t/// This function queries workers from tmux and associates them with their\n426\t/// corresponding workspaces based on session naming patterns and workspace paths.\n427\t///\n428\t/// Session names that include workspace context:\n429\t/// - `claude-code-glm-47-alpha` → current workspace\n430\t/// - `claude-code-glm-47-alpha@forge` → workspace \"forge\"\n431\t///\n432\t/// # Example\n433\t///\n434\t/// ```no_run\n435\t/// use forge_worker::discovery::discover_workers_cross_workspace;\n436\t/// use forge_core::WorkspaceRegistry;\n437\t///\n438\t/// #[tokio::main]\n439\t/// async fn main() -> forge_core::Result<()> {\n440\t///     let registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n441\t///     let result = discover_workers_cross_workspace(&registry).await?;\n442\t///\n443\t///     println!(\"Found {} workers across {} workspaces\",\n444\t///         result.workers.len(),\n445\t///         result.by_workspace.len()\n446\t///     );\n447\t///\n448\t///     Ok(())\n449\t/// }\n450\t/// ```\n451\t#[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip_all)]\n452\tpub async fn discover_workers_cross_workspace(\n453\t    registry: &WorkspaceRegistry,\n454\t) -> Result<CrossWorkspaceDiscoveryResult> {\n455\t    let base_result = discover_workers().await?;\n456\t\n457\t    let mut result = CrossWorkspaceDiscoveryResult::default();\n458\t    result.by_type = base_result.by_type;\n459\t    result.total_attached = base_result.attached_count;\n460\t    result.total_detached = base_result.detached_count;\n461\t\n462\t    // If no workspaces configured, treat all workers as belonging to \"default\"\n463\t    if registry.is_empty() {\n464\t        for worker in base_result.workers {\n465\t            let ws_worker = WorkspaceWorker {\n466\t                workspace_id: \"default\".to_string(),\n467\t                workspace_name: \"Default\".to_string(),\n468\t                workspace_path: std::env::current_dir()\n469\t                    .unwrap_or_default()\n470\t                    .display()\n471\t                    .to_string(),\n472\t                worker,\n473\t            };\n474\t            result.by_workspace\n475\t                .entry(\"default\".to_string())\n476\t                .or_default()\n477\t                .push(ws_worker.clone());\n478\t            result.workers.push(ws_worker);\n479\t        }\n480\t        return Ok(result);\n481\t    }\n482\t\n483\t    // Associate workers with workspaces\n484\t    for worker in base_result.workers {\n485\t        let workspace_id = extract_workspace_from_session(&worker.session_name)\n486\t            .or_else(|| {\n487\t                // Try to match by current workspace\n488\t                registry.current().map(|ws| ws.id.clone())\n489\t            })\n490\t            .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n491\t                // Fall back to first enabled workspace\n492\t                registry\n493\t                    .enabled()\n494\t                    .first()\n495\t                    .map(|ws| ws.id.clone())\n496\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|| \"unknown\".to_string())\n497\t            });\n498\t\n499\t        let workspace = registry.get(&workspace_id);\n500\t\n501\t        let ws_worker = WorkspaceWorker {\n502\t            workspace_id: workspace_id.clone(),\n503\t            workspace_name: workspace\n504\t                .map(|ws| ws.name.clone())\n505\t                .unwrap_or_else(|| workspace_id.clone()),\n506\t            workspace_path: workspace\n507\t                .map(|ws| ws.path.display().to_string())\n508\t                .unwrap_or_default(),\n509\t            worker,\n510\t        };\n511\t\n512\t        result\n513\t            .by_workspace\n514\t            .entry(workspace_id.clone())\n515\t            .or_default()\n516\t            .push(ws_worker.clone());\n517\t        result.workers.push(ws_worker);\n518\t    }\n519\t\n520\t    debug!(\n521\t        \"Discovered {} workers across {} workspaces\",\n522\t        result.workers.len(),\n523\t        result.by_workspace.len()\n524\t    );\n525\t\n526\t    Ok(result)\n527\t}\n528\t\n529\t/// Extract workspace ID from a session name.\n530\t///\n531\t/// Supports patterns like:\n532\t/// - `claude-code-glm-47-alpha@forge` → \"forge\"\n533\t/// - `claude-code-glm-47-alpha` → None (use default)\n534\tfn extract_workspace_from_session(session_name: &str) -> Option<String> {\n535\t    if let Some(at_pos) = session_name.rfind('@') {\n536\t        let workspace = session_name[at_pos + 1..].to_string();\n537\t        if !workspace.is_empty() {\n538\t            return Some(workspace);\n539\t        }\n540\t    }\n541\t    None\n542\t}\n543\t\n544\t/// Discover workers for a specific workspace by path.\n545\t///\n546\t/// This checks the workspace's status directory for worker status files\n547\t/// and cross-references with tmux sessions.\n548\t#[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip_all, fields(workspace_path = %workspace_path.display()))]\n549\tpub async fn discover_workers_for_workspace(workspace_path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<DiscoveredWorker>> {\n550\t    let status_dir = workspace_path.join(\".forge\").join(\"status\");\n551\t\n552\t    if !status_dir.exists() {\n553\t        debug!(\"No status directory for workspace: {:?}\", workspace_path);\n554\t        return Ok(Vec::new());\n555\t    }\n556\t\n557\t    // Read worker status files\n558\t    let mut worker_ids = Vec::new();\n559\t    let mut entries = tokio::fs::read_dir(&status_dir).await.map_err(|e| {\n560\t        ForgeError::WorkerSpawn {\n561\t            worker_id: \"discovery\".into(),\n562\t            message: format!(\"Failed to read status directory {:?}: {}\", status_dir, e),\n563\t        }\n564\t    })?;\n565\t\n566\t    while let Some(entry) = entries.next_entry().await.map_err(|e| {\n567\t        ForgeError::WorkerSpawn {\n568\t            worker_id: \"discovery\".into(),\n569\t            message: format!(\"Failed to read status directory entry: {}\", e),\n570\t        }\n571\t    })? {\n572\t        let path = entry.path();\n573\t        if path.extension().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) == Some(\"json\") {\n574\t            if let Some(stem) = path.file_stem().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) {\n575\t                worker_ids.push(stem.to_string());\n576\t            }\n577\t        }\n578\t    }\n579\t\n580\t    // Get all tmux sessions and filter by workspace workers\n581\t    let all_workers = discover_workers().await?;\n582\t    let workspace_workers: Vec<DiscoveredWorker> = all_workers\n583\t        .workers\n584\t        .into_iter()\n585\t        .filter(|w| worker_ids.contains(&w.session_name))\n586\t        .collect();\n587\t\n588\t    debug!(\n589\t        \"Found {} workers for workspace {:?}\",\n590\t        workspace_workers.len(),\n591\t        workspace_path\n592\t    );\n593\t\n594\t    Ok(workspace_workers)\n595\t}\n596\t\n597\t/// Get worker count summary for all workspaces.\n598\t///\n599\t/// Returns a map of workspace_id -> layout based on available screen real estate.\n178\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n179\tpub enum LayoutMode {\n180\t    /// Ultra-wide layout (199+ cols): 3-column with all 6 panels visible.\n181\t    /// Left (66 cols): Workers + Subscriptions\n182\t    /// Middle (66 cols): Tasks + Activity\n183\t    /// Right (65 cols): Costs + Actions\n184\t    UltraWide,\n185\t    /// Wide layout (120-198 cols): 2-column with 4 panels visible.\n186\t    Wide,\n187\t    /// Narrow layout (<120 cols): single-view mode.\n188\t    Narrow,\n189\t}\n190\t\n191\timpl LayoutMode {\n192\t    /// Determine the layout mode based on terminal width.\n193\t    pub fn from_width(width: u16) -> Self {\n194\t        if width >= 199 {\n195\t            LayoutMode::UltraWide\n196\t        } else if width >= 120 {\n197\t            LayoutMode::Wide\n198\t        } else {\n199\t            LayoutMode::Narrow\n200\t        }\n201\t    }\n202\t\n203\t    /// Get the minimum terminal height for this layout mode.\n204\t    pub fn min_height(&self) -> u16 {\n205\t        match self {\n206\t            LayoutMode::UltraWide => 38,\n207\t            LayoutMode::Wide => 30,\n208\t            LayoutMode::Narrow => 20,\n209\t        }\n210\t    }\n211\t\n212\t    /// Check if the terminal dimensions meet the layout requirements.\n213\t    pub fn meets_requirements(&self, width: u16, height: u16) -> bool {\n214\t        let required_mode = Self::from_width(width);\n215\t        let height_ok = height >= self.min_height();\n216\t\n217\t        // Mode is valid if it matches width requirement and height is sufficient\n218\t        *self == required_mode && height_ok\n219\t    }\n220\t}\n221\t\n222\t#[cfg(test)]\n223\tmod tests {\n224\t    use super::*;\n225\t\n226\t {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_3b56b207c47440528e6cbaa2","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Main application state and logic for the FORGE TUI.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! The `App` struct manages overall application state, view switching,\n4\t//! and coordinates between different components.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! # Chat Backend Integration Architecture\n7\t//!\n8\t//! The chat functionality integrates an async `ChatBackend` into the synchronous\n9\t//! TUI event loop using the following design:\n10\t//!\n11\t//! ## Async-to-Sync Bridge\n12\t//!\n13\t//! 1. **Tokio Runtime**: A single-threaded tokio runtime is created during App::new()\n14\t//!    and lives for the duration of the application. This runtime handles all async\n15\t//!    chat operations.\n16\t//!\n17\t//! 2. **Message Passing**: An `mpsc` channel connects async chat tasks to the sync UI:\n18\t//!    - `chat_tx`: Sender held by App, used to submit chat requests\n19\t//!    - `chat_rx`: Receiver held by a background task, processes requests\n20\t//!    - `response_rx`: Receiver held by App, receives responses\n21\t//!\n22\t//! 3. **Non-Blocking Execution**: When user submits chat input:\n23\t//!    - Send request to background task via `chat_tx`\n24\t//!    - Set `chat_pending` flag and display loading indicator\n25\t//!    - Continue UI event loop without blocking\n26\t//!    - Poll `response_rx` each frame to check for responses\n27\t//!\n28\t//! ## State Management\n29\t//!\n30\t//! - `chat_pending: bool` - Whether a request is in flight\n31\t//! - `chat_history: Vec<ChatExchange>` - Last 10 exchanges (user query + response)\n32\t//! - `chat_backend: Option<ChatBackend>` - Initialized from config.yaml\n33\t//!\n34\t//! ## Error Handling\n35\t//!\n36\t//! - Backend initialization failures: App runs without chat (display error in UI)\n37\t//! - Request timeout: Background task implements 30s timeout\n38\t//! - API errors: Captured in response, displayed in chat history as error messages\n39\t//!\n40\t//! ## Performance\n41\t//!\n42\t//! - Background task prevents UI blocking\n43\t//! - Response polling adds <1ms per frame\n44\t//! - Chat history limited to 10 exchanges (~1KB memory)\n45\t\n46\tuse std::io;\n47\tuse std::path::PathBuf;\n48\tuse    /// Provider name\n370\t    #[serde(default)]\n371\t    pub provider: String,\n372\t}\n373\t\n374\t/// Generate actionable guidance for common errors.\n375\tfn get_error_guidance(error_message: &str) -> Option<String> {\n376\t    let error_lower = error_message.to_lowercase();\n377\t\n378\t    // Config not found errors\n379\t    if error_lower.contains(\"config\") && (error_lower.contains(\"not found\") || error_lower.contains(\"missing\")) {\n380\t        return Some(\"Run 'forge init' to create a configuration file, or check that ~/.forge/config.yaml exists.\".to_string());\n381\t    }\n382\t\n383\t    // API/authentication errors\n384\t    if error_lower.contains(\"api key\") || error_lower.contains(\"authentication\") || migrations.\n16\tconst SCHEMA_VERSION: i32 = 3;\n17\t\n18\t/// Maximum retries for database lock errors.\n19\tconst DB_LOCK_MAX_RETRIES: u32 = 5;\n20\t\n21\t/// Initial delay for database lock retry (in milliseconds).\n22\tconst DB_LOCK_INITIAL_DELAY_MS: u64 = 100;\n23\t\n24\t/// Maximum delay for database lock retry.\n25\tconst DB_LOCK_MAX_DELAY: Duration = Duration::from_secs(5);\n26\t\n27\t/// SQLite database for cost tracking.\n28\tpub struct CostDatabase {\n29\t    conn: Arc<Mutex<Connection>>,\n30\t}\n31\t\n32\timpl CostDatabase {\n33\t    /// Open or create a cost database at the given path.\n34\t    pub fn open<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<Self> {\n35\t        let conn = Connection::open(path)?;\n36\t        let db = Self {\n37\t            conn: Arc::new(Mutex::new(conn)),\n38\t        };\n39\t        db.migrate()?;\n40\t        Ok(db)\n41\t    }\n42\t\n43\t    /// Create an in-memory database (for testing).\n44\t    pub fn open_in_memory() -> Result<Self> {\n45\t        let conn = Connection::open_in_memory()?;\n46\t        let db = Self {\n47\t            conn: Arc::new(Mutex::new(conn)),\n48\t        };\n49\t        db.migrate()?;\n50\t        Ok(db)\n51\t    }\n52\t\n53\t    /// Execute a database operation with automatic retry on lock errors.\n54\t    ///\n55\t    /// This helper wraps database operations that may fail due to concurrent\n56\t    /// access, automatically retrying with exponential backoff.\n57\t    fn with_retry<T, F>(&self, operation: &str, mut f: F) -> Result<T>\n58\t    where\n59\t        F: FnMut() -> Result<T>,\n60\t    {\n61\t        let mut attempt = 0;\n62\t        let mut delay = Duration::from_millis(DB_LOCK_INITIAL_DELAY_MS);\n63\t\n64\t        loop {\n65\t            attempt += 1;\n66\t\n67\t            match f() {\n68\t                Ok(result) => {\n69\t                    if attempt > 1 {\n70\t                        info!(\n71\t                            attempt,\n72\t                            operation,\n73\t                            \"Database operation succeeded after retry\"\n74\t                        );\n75\t                    }\n76\t                    return Ok(result);\n77\t                }\n78\t                Err(ref e) if is_database_locked_error(e) && attempt <= DB_LOCK_MAX_RETRIES => {\n79\t                    warn!(\n80\t                        attempt,\n81\t                        max_retries = DB_LOCK_MAX_RETRIES,\n82\t                        delay_ms = delay.as_millis(),\n83\t                        operation,\n84\t                        \"Database locked, retrying with backoff\"\n85\t                    );\n86\t\n87\t                    std::thread::sleep(delay);\n88\t\n89\t                    // Exponential backoff with cap\n90\t                    delay = std::cmp::min(delay * 2, DB_LOCK_MAX_DELAY);\n91\t                }\n92\t                Err(e) => {\n93\t                    if attempt > 1 {\n94\t                        warn!(\n95\t                            attempt,\n96\t                            operation,\n97\t                            error = %e,\n98\t                            \"Database operation failed after retries\"\n99\t                        );\n100\t                    }\n101\t                    return Err(e);\n102\t                }\n103\t            }\n104\t        }\n105\t    }\n106\t\n107\t    /// Run database migrations.\n108\t    fn migrate(&self) -> Result<()> {\n109\t        let conn = self\n110\t            .conn\n111\t            .lock()\n112\t            .map_err(|e| CostError::Migration(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e)))?;\n113\t\n114\t        // Create schema version table\n115\t        conn.execute(\n116\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (\n117\t                version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY\n118\t            )\",\n119\t            [],\n120\t        )?;\n121\t\n122\t        // Get current version\n123\t        let current_version: i32 = conn\n124\t            .query_row(\n125\t                \"SELECT COALESCE(MAX(version), 0) FROM schema_version\",\n126\t                [],\n127\t                |row| row.get(0),\n128\t            )\n129\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n130\t\n131\t        if current_version < SCHEMA_VERSION {\n132\t            info!(\n133\t                current = Workspace Concept and Root Definition\n\n### How Workspace Root is Defined\n**Location**: `/home/coding/FORGE/crates/forge-core/src/workspace.rs`\n\n**Key Structure**:\n- **WorkspaceConfig** (lines 15-84): Defines a workspace with:\n  - `id`: Unique identifier (e.g., \"forge\", \"myproject\")\n  - `name`: Human-readable name\n  - `path`: Absolute path to workspace root (PathBuf)\n  - `enabled`: Whether actively monitored\n  - `priority`: Display ordering (lower = higher priority)\n  - `description`: Optional description\n\n**Workspace Root Detection**:\n- A directory is considered a workspace if it contains a `.forge` subdirectory (line 251-266)\n- Helper methods for workspace paths:\n  - `status_dir()` → `.forge/status`\n  - `logs_dir()` → `.forge/logs`\n  - `cost_db_path()` → `.forge/costs.db`\n  - `beads_dir()` → `.beads`\n\n### WorkspaceRegistry\n**Lines 87-392**: Central registry managing multiple workspaces\n- Stores workspaces in a `Vec<WorkspaceConfig>` with HashMap index for fast lookup\n- Key methods:\n  - `add/remove`: Modify registry\n  - `get/get_mut`: Retrieve by ID\n  - `enabled()`: Filter enabled workspaces\n  - `current()`: Get first enabled (by priority)\n  - `set_current(id)`: Change current workspace\n  - `load_from_file(path)`: Load YAML registry\n  - `save_to_file(path)`: Persist registry\n\n**Current Workspace Selection**:\n- Determined by priority (lowest number first)\n- Default priority is 100\n- `set_current()` moves workspace to priority 0 and shifts others\n\n## 2. Worker Discovery Mechanism\n\n**Location**: `/home/coding/FORGE/crates/forge-worker/src/discovery.rs`\n\n### Discovery Pattern (lines 1-369)\n\n**WorkerType Enum** (lines 48-106):\n- Parses worker type from tmux session names\n- Types: `Glm47`, `Sonnet`, `Opus`, `Haiku`, `Unknown`\n- Pattern matching: `claude-code-glm-47-*`, `claude-code-sonnet-*`, etc.\n\n**DiscoveredWorker Structure** (lines 114-158):\n- `session_name`: tmux session identifier\n- `worker_type`: Parsed worker type\n- `created_at/last_activity`: Timestamps\n- `is_attached`: Whether client attached\n- `suffix`: Worker suffix (alpha, bravo, charlie)\n- `executor`: Executor prefix\n\n**Main Discovery Function** (lines 267-330):\n```rust\npub async fn discover_workers() -> Result<DiscoveryResult>\n```\n- Runs `tmux list-sessions` with custom format\n- Parses output: `#{session_name}:#{session_created}:#{session_activity}:#{session_attached}`\n- Filters to known worker prefixes (lines 202-208)\n- Returns statistics by type, attached/detached counts\n\n### Cross-Workspace Discovery (lines 365-612)\n\n**WorkspaceWorker Structure** (lines 369-380):\n- Wraps `DiscoveredWorker` with workspace context\n- Adds `workspace_id`, `workspace_name`, `workspace_path`\n\n**Key Function** (lines 451-527):\n```rust\npub async fn discover_workers_cross_workspace(\n    registry: &WorkspaceRegistry,\n) -> Result<CrossWorkspaceDiscoveryResult>\n```\n\n**Workspace Association Strategy** (lines 484-497):\n1. Extract workspace from session name suffix (`@workspace-id`)\n2. Fall back to current workspace from registry\n3. Fall back to first enabled workspace\n4. Default to \"unknown\" if no workspaces configured\n\n**Session Naming Convention**:\n- `claude-code-glm-47-alpha` → current/default workspace\n- `claude-code-glm-47-alpha@forge` → workspace \"forge\"\n\n**Helper Functions**:\n- `discover_workers_for_workspace(path)` (lines 545-595): Discover workers for specific workspace\n- `get_workspace_worker_counts(registry)` (lines 598-611): Get counts overview\n\n## 3. Cost Tracking Architecture\n\n**Location**: `/home/coding/FORGE/crates/forge-cost/`\n\n### Cost Storage Structure (db.rs, models.rs)\n\n**Database Schema** (lines 159-200 in db.rs):\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE api_calls (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,\n    worker_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id TEXT,\n    model TEXT NOT NULL,\n    input_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    output_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    cache_creation_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    cache_read_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    cost_usd REAL NOT NULL,\n    bead_id TEXT,\n    event_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'result'\n)\n```\n\n**Indexes**:\n- `idx_api_calls_timestamp`: For date range queries\n- `idx_api_calls_worker`: For worker-specific queries\n- `idx_api_calls_model`: For model breakdowns\n\n### Cost Query Patterns (query.rs)\n\n**CostQuery Interface** (lines 12-195):\n```rust\npub struct CostQuery<'a> {\n    db: &'a CostDatabase,\n}\n```\n\n**Key Query Methods**:\n- `get_today_costs()`: Today's aggregated costs\n- `get_costs_for_date(date)`: Specific date with model breakdown\n- `get_monthly_costs(year, month)`: Monthly aggregation\n- `get_weekly_costs()`: Last 7 days\n\n**Query Pattern**:\n1. Try aggregation table (`daily_costs`) first\n2. Fall back to raw `api_calls` table\n3. Group by model for breakdown\n4. Calculate totals and percentages\n\n### Multi-Workspace Cost Aggregation (multi_workspace.rs)\n\n**WorkspaceCost Structure** (lines 16-32):\n- `workspace_id/name`: Workspace identifiers\n- `workspace_path`: Path to workspace\n- `has_cost_data`: Boolean flag\n- `today_cost`: Optional daily cost\n- `total_cost`: Total cost\n- `worker_count`: Worker count\n\n**MultiWorkspaceCostAggregator** (lines 73-331):\n```rust\npub struct MultiWorkspaceCostAggregator {\n    workspace_dbs: HashMap<String, PathBuf>,\n}\n```\n\n**Creation Methods**:\n- `new(workspace_dbs)`: Direct creation\n- `from_workspace_paths(workspaces)`: Auto-discover cost DBs\n\n**Key Methods**:\n- `aggregate_today_costs()`: Today's costs across all workspaces\n- `aggregate_costs_for_date(date)`: Specific date\n- `aggregate_costs_range(start, end)`: Date range\n- `get_workspace_breakdown_today()`: Per-workspace breakdown\n- `aggregate_model_costs(start, end)`: By model across workspaces\n\n**Helper Functions**:\n- `workspace_cost_db_path(path)` → `path/.forge/costs.db`\n- `workspace_has_cost_data(path)`: Check if DB exists\n\n## 4. Views represent the different screens/modes available in the dashboard.\n4\t\n5\tuse std::fmt;\n6\t\n7\t/// Available views in the FORGE dashboard.\n8\t///\n9\t/// Each view represents a distinct screen with its own content and interactions.\n10\t/// Views can be switched using hotkeys or the Tab key to cycle.\n11\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]\n12\tpub enum View {\n13\t    /// Overview/dashboard showing summary of all components\n14\t    #[default]\n15\t    Overview,\n16\t    /// Worker pool management and status\n17\t    Workers,\n18\t    /// Task queue and alerts and notifications\n31\t    Alerts,\n32\t    /// Model routing analytics and complexity scoring\n33\t    Routing,\n34\t    /// Conversational chat interface (activated with `:`)\n35\t    Chat,\n36\t    /// Multi-workspace management and switching\n37\t    Workspaces,\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\timpl View {\n41\t    /// Returns the hotkey character for this view.\n42\t    pub fn hotkey(&self) -> char {\n43\t        match self {\n44\t            View::Overview => 'o',\n45\t            View::Workers => 'w',\n46\t            View::Tasks => 't',\n47\t            View::Costs => 'c',\n48\t            View::Metrics => 'm',\n49\t            View::Perf => 'p',\n50\t            View::Logs => 'l',\n51\t            View::Subscriptions => 'u',\n52\t            View::Alerts => 'a',\n53\t fn title(&self) -> &'static str {\n61\t        match self {\n62\t            View::Overview => \"Overview\",\n63\t            View::Workers => \"Workers\",\n64\t            View::Tasks => \"Tasks\",\n65\t            View::Costs => \"Costs\",\n66\t            View::Metrics => \"Metrics\",\n67\t            View::Perf => \"Perf\",\n68\t            View::Logs => \"Logs\",\n69\t            View::Subscriptions => \"Subscriptions\",\n70\t            View::Alerts => \"Alerts\",\n71\t            View::Routing => \"Routing\",\n72\t            View::Chat => \"Chat\",\n73\t            View::Workspaces => \"Workspaces\",\n74\t        }\n75\t    }\n76\t\n77\t    /// Returns the hotkey hint for status bar display.\n78\t    pub fn hotkey_hint(&self) -> String {\n79\t        format!(\"[{}] {}\", self.hotkey(), self.title())\n80\t    }\n81\t\n82\t    /// All views in display order (for Tab cycling).\n83\t    pub const ALL: [View; 12] = [\n84\t        View::Overview,\n85\t        View::Workers,\n86\t        View::Tasks,\n87\t        View::Costs,\n88\t        View::Metrics,\n89\t        View::Perf,\n90\t        View::Logs,\n91\t        View::Subscriptions,\n92\t        View::Alerts,\n93\t        View::Routing,\n94\t        View::Chat,\n95\t        View::Workspaces,\n96\t    ];\n97\t\n98\t    /// Returns the next view in the cycle (for Tab navigation).\n99\t    pub fn next(&self) -> View {\n100\t        let idx = Self::ALL.iter().position(|v| v == self).unwrap_or(0);\n101\t        Self::ALL[(idx + 1) % Self::ALL.len()]\n102\t    }\n103\t\n104\t    /// Returns the previous view in the cycle (for Shift+Tab navigation).\n105\t    pub fn prev(&self) -> View {\n106\t        let idx = Self::ALL.iter().position(|v| v == self).unwrap_or(0);\n107\t        if idx == 0 {\n108\t            Self::ALL[Self::ALL.len() - 1]\n109\t        } else {\n110\t            Self::ALL[idx - 1]\n111\t        }\n112\t    }\n113\t\n114\t    /// Try to parse a view from a hotkey character.\n115\t    pub fn from_hotkey(key: char) -> Option<View> {\n116\t        match key {\n117\t            'o' | 'O' => Some(View::Overview),\n118\t            'w' => Some(View::Workers), // lowercase only\n119\t            'W' => Some(View::Workspaces), // uppercase only\n120\t            't' | 'T' => Some(View::Tasks),\n121\t            'c' | 'C' => Some(View::Costs),\n122\t            'm' | 'M' => Some(View::Metrics),\n123\t            'p' | 'P' => Some(View::Perf),\n124\t            'l' | 'L' => Some(View::Logs),\n125\t            'u' | 'U' => Some(View::Subscriptions),\n126\t            'a' | 'A' => Some(View::Alerts),\n127\t            'r' | 'R' => Some(View::Routing),\n128\t            ':' => Some(View::Chat),\n129\t            _ => None,\n130\t        }\n131\t    }\n132\t}\n133\t\n134\timpl fmt::Display for View {\n135\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n136\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.title())\n137\t    }\n138\t}\n139\t\n140\t/// Focus state for panels within a view.\n141\t///\n142\t/// Tracks which panel has keyboard focus for navigation and highlighting.\n143\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]\n144\tpub enum FocusPanel {\n145\t    /// No specific panel focused (view-level focus)\n146\t    #[default]\n147\t    None,\n148\t    /// Worker pool panel\n149\t    WorkerPool,\n150\t    /// Subscription/usage panel\n151\t    Subscriptions,\n152\t    /// Task queue panel\n153\t    TaskQueue,\n154\t    /// Activity log panel\n155\t    ActivityLog,\n156\t    ///  ChatInput,\n164\t    /// Alert list panel\n165\t    AlertList,\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\timpl FocusPanel {\n169\t    /// Returns whether this panel should show a highlight border.\n170\t    pub fn is_highlighted(&self) -> bool {\n171\t        !matches!(self, FocusPanel::None)\n172\t    }\n173\t}\n174\t\n175\t/// Layout mode based on terminal dimensions.\n176\t///\n177\t/// The TUI adapts its layout based on available screen real estate.\n178\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n179\tpub enum LayoutMode {\n180\t    /// Ultra-wide layout (199+ cols): 3-column with all 6 panels visible.\n181\t    /// Left (66 cols): Workers + Subscriptions\n182\t    /// Middle (66 cols): Tasks + Activity\n183\t    /// Right (65 cols): Costs + Actions\n184\t    UltraWide,\n185\t    /// Wide layout (120-198 cols): 2-column with 4 panels visible.\n186\t    Wide,\n187\t    /// Narrow layout (<120 cols): single-view mode.\n188\t    Narrow,\n189\t}\n190\t\n191\timpl LayoutMode {\n192\t    /// Determine the layout mode based on terminal width.\n193\t    pub fn from_width(width: u16) -> Self {\n194\t        if width >= 199 {\n195\t            LayoutMode::UltraWide\n196\t        } else if width >= 120 {\n197\t            LayoutMode::Wide\n198\t        } else {\n199\t            LayoutMode::Narrow\n200\t        }\n201\t    }\n202\t\n203\t    /// Get the minimum terminal height for this layout mode.\n204\t    pub fn min_height(&self) -> u16 {\n205\t        match self {\n206\t            LayoutMode::UltraWide => 38,\n207\t            LayoutMode::Wide => 30,\n208\t            LayoutMode::Narrow => 20,\n209\t        }\n210\t    }\n211\t\n212\t    /// Check if the terminal dimensions meet the layout requirements.\n213\t    pub fn meets_requirements(&self, width: u16, height: u16) -> bool {\n214\t        let required_mode = Self::from_width(width);\n215\t        let height_ok = height >= self.min_height();\n216\t\n217\t        // Mode is valid if it matches width requirement and height is sufficient\n218\t        *self == required_mode && height_ok\n219\t    }\n220\t}\n221\t\n222\t#[cfg(test)]\n223\tmod tests {\n224\t    use super::*;\n225\t\n226\t View types and navigation for the FORGE TUI.\n//!\n//! Views represent the different screens/modes available in the dashboard.\n\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Available views in the FORGE dashboard.\n///\n/// Each view represents a distinct screen with its own content and interactions.\n/// Views can be switched using hotkeys or the Tab key to cycle.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]\npub enum View {\n    /// Overview/dashboard showing summary of all components\n    #[default]\n    Overview,\n    /// Worker pool management and status\n    Workers,\n    /// Task queue and bead management\n    Tasks,\n    /// Cost analytics and optimization\n    Costs,\n    /// Performance metrics and statistics\n    Metrics,\n    /// FORGE internal performance (event loop, render time, memory)\n    Perf,\n    /// Activity log viewer\n    Logs,\n    /// Subscription tracking and utilization\n    Subscriptions,\n    /// Worker health alerts and notifications\n    Alerts,\n    /// Model routing analytics and complexity scoring\n    Routing,\n    /// Conversational chat interface (activated with `:`)\n    Chat,\n    /// Multi-workspace management and switching\n    Workspaces,\n}\n\nimpl View {\n    /// Returns the hotkey character for this view.\n    pub fn hotkey(&self) -> char {\n        match self {\n            View::Overview => 'o',\n            View::Workers => 'w',\n            View::Tasks => 't',\n            View::Costs => 'c',\n            View::Metrics => 'm',\n            View::Perf => 'p',\n            View::Logs => 'l',\n            View::Subscriptions => 'u',\n            View::Alerts => 'a',\n            View::Routing => 'r',\n            View::Chat => ':',\n            View::Workspaces => 'W',\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the display title for this view.\n    pub fn title(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            View::Overview => \"Overview\",\n            View::Workers => \"Workers\",\n            View::Tasks => \"Tasks\",\n            View::Costs => \"Costs\",\n            View::Metrics => \"Metrics\",\n            View::Perf => \"Perf\",\n            View::Logs => \"Logs\",\n            View::Subscriptions => \"Subscriptions\",\n            View::Alerts => \"Alerts\",\n            View::Routing => \"Routing\",\n            View::Chat => \"Chat\",\n            View::Workspaces => \"Workspaces\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the hotkey hint for status bar display.\n    pub fn hotkey_hint(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\"[{}] {}\", self.hotkey(), self.title())\n    }\n\n    /// All views in display order (for Tab cycling).\n    pub const ALL: [View; 12] = [\n        View::Overview,\n        View::Workers,\n        View::Tasks,\n        View::Costs,\n        View::Metrics,\n        View::Perf,\n        View::Logs,\n        View::Subscriptions,\n        View::Alerts,\n        View::Routing,\n        View::Chat,\n        View::Workspaces,\n    ];\n\n    /// Returns the next view in the cycle (for Tab navigation).\n    pub fn next(&self) -> View {\n        let idx = Self::ALL.iter().position(|v| v == self).unwrap_or(0);\n        Self::ALL[(idx + 1) % Self::ALL.len()]\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the previous view in the cycle (for Shift+Tab navigation).\n    pub fn prev(&self) -> View {\n        let idx = Self::ALL.iter().position(|v| v == self).unwrap_or(0);\n        if idx == 0 {\n            Self::ALL[Self::ALL.len() - 1]\n        } else {\n            Self::ALL[idx - 1]\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Try to parse a view from a hotkey character.\n    pub fn from_hotkey(key: char) -> Option<View> {\n        match key {\n            'o' | 'O' => Some(View::Overview),\n            'w' => Some(View::Workers), // lowercase only\n            'W' => Some(View::Workspaces), // uppercase only\n            't' | 'T' => Some(View::Tasks),\n            'c' | 'C' => Some(View::Costs),\n            'm' | 'M' => Some(View::Metrics),\n            'p' | 'P' => Some(View::Perf),\n            'l' | 'L' => Some(View::Logs),\n            'u' | 'U' => Some(View::Subscriptions),\n            'a' | 'A' => Some(View::Alerts),\n            'r' | 'R' => Some(View::Routing),\n            ':' => Some(View::Chat),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for View {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}\", self.title())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Focus state for panels within a view.\n///\n/// Tracks which panel has keyboard focus for navigation and highlighting.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]\npub enum FocusPanel {\n    /// No specific panel focused (view-level focus)\n    #[default]\n    None,\n    /// Worker pool panel\n    WorkerPool,\n    /// Subscription/usage panel\n    Subscriptions,\n    /// Task queue panel\n    TaskQueue,\n    /// Activity log panel\n    ActivityLog,\n    /// Cost breakdown panel\n    CostBreakdown,\n    /// Metrics charts panel\n    MetricsCharts,\n    /// Perf metrics panel\n    PerfCharts,\n    /// Chat input panel\n    ChatInput,\n    /// Alert list panel\n    AlertList,\n}\n\nimpl FocusPanel {\n    /// Returns whether this panel should show a highlight border.\n    pub fn is_highlighted(&self) -> bool {\n        !matches!(self, FocusPanel::None)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Layout mode based on terminal dimensions.\n///\n/// The TUI adapts its layout based on available screen real estate.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum LayoutMode {\n    /// Ultra-wide layout (199+ cols): 3-column with all 6 panels visible.\n    /// Left (66 cols): Workers + Subscriptions\n    /// Middle (66 cols): Tasks + Activity\n    /// Right (65 cols): Costs + Actions\n    UltraWide,\n    /// Wide layout (120-198 cols): 2-column with 4 panels visible.\n    Wide,\n    /// Narrow layout (<120 cols): single-view mode.\n    Narrow,\n}\n\nimpl LayoutMode {\n    /// Determine the layout mode based on terminal width.\n    pub fn from_width(width: u16) -> Self {\n        if width >= 199 {\n            LayoutMode::UltraWide\n        } else if width >= 120 {\n            LayoutMode::Wide\n        } else {\n            LayoutMode::Narrow\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the minimum terminal height for this layout mode.\n    pub fn min_height(&self) -> u16 {\n        match self {\n            LayoutMode::UltraWide => 38,\n            LayoutMode::Wide => 30,\n            LayoutMode::Narrow => 20,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the terminal dimensions meet the layout requirements.\n    pub fn meets_requirements(&self, width: u16, height: u16) -> bool {\n        let required_mode = Self::from_width(width);\n        let height_ok = height >= self.min_height();\n\n        // Mode is valid if it matches width requirement and height is sufficient\n        *self == required_mode && height_ok\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_view_hotkeys() }\n71\t}\n72\t\n73\t/// Aggregator for costs across multiple workspaces.\n74\tpub struct MultiWorkspaceCostAggregator {\n75\t    /// Path to each workspace's cost database\n76\t    workspace_dbs: `.forge/costs.db` file.\n88\t    pub fn from_workspace_paths(\n89\t        workspaces: HashMap<String, (String, PathBuf)>,\n90\t    ) -> Result<Self> {\n91\t        let mut workspace_dbs = /// Aggregate costs for a specific date across all workspaces.\n123\t    pub fn aggregate_costs_for_date(&self, date: NaiveDate) -> Result<MultiWorkspaceCostSummary> {\n124\t        let mut summary = daily_cost.total_cost_usd;\n132\t                    let ws_cost = WorkspaceCost {\n133\t                        workspace_id: workspace_id.clone(),\n134\t                        workspace_name: workspace_name.clone(),\n135\t                        workspace_path: db_path\n136\t                            .parent()\n137\t                            .and_then(|p| p.parent())\n138\t                            .unwrap_or(db_path)\n139\t                            .to_path_buf(),\n140\t                        has_cost_data: true,\n141\t                        today_cost: summary.by_workspace.insert(workspace_id.clone(), ws_cost);\n150\t                }\n151\t                Ok(None) => {\n152\t                    // Workspace has no cost data for this date\n153\t         worker_count: 0,\n168\t                        },\n169\t                    );\n170\t                }\n171\t                Err(e) => {\n172\t                    warn!(\"Failed to query cost for workspace {}: {}\", workspace_id, e);\n173\t                    summary.workspaces_without_costs += 1;\n174\t                }\n175\t            }\n176\t        }\n177\t\n178\t   Ok(summary)\n185\t    }\n186\t\n187\t    /// Aggregate costs across all workspaces for a date range.\n188\t    pub fn aggregate_costs_range(\n189\t        &self,\n190\t        start_date:     }\n224\t\n225\t        Ok(summary)\n226\t    }\n227\t\n228\t    /// Query cost for a specific workspace on a specific date.\n229\t    fn query_workspace_cost(\n230\t        &self,\n231\t  Ok(Some(daily))\n241\t                } else {\n242\t                    Ok(None)\n243\t                }\n244\t            }\n245\t            Err(e) => {\n246\t                // Treat query errors }\n252\t\n253\t    /// Query cost for a specific workspace over a date range.\n254\t    fn query_workspace_cost_range(\n255\t        &self,\n256\t        db_path: &Path,\n257\t        start_date: NaiveDate,\n258\t        end_date: NaiveDate,\n259\t    ) -> Result<f64> {\n260\t        let db = CostDatabase::open(db_path)?;\n261\t        let conn = db.connection();\n262\t        let conn = Result<HashMap<String, f64>> {\n288\t        let mut model_costs: HashMap<String, f64> = HashMap::new();\n289\t\n290\t        for db_path in self.workspace_dbs.values() {\n291\t            let db =
