    }\n299\t                }\n300\t                Err(e) => {\n301\t                    warn!(\"Failed to get model costs for {:?}: {}\", db_path, e);\n302\t                }\n303\t            }\n304\t        }\n305\t\n306\t        Ok(model_costs)\n307\t    }\n308\t\n309\t    /// Get cost breakdown by workspace for today.\n310\t    pub fn get_workspace_breakdown_today(&self) -> Cross-workspace cost aggregation.\n//!\n//! This module provides utilities for querying and aggregating costs across\n//! multiple FORGE workspaces, each with its own cost database.\n\nuse crate::db::CostDatabase;\nuse crate::error::{CostError, Result};\nuse crate::models::DailyCost;\nuse chrono::{NaiveDate, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn};\n\n/// Cost information for a single workspace.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WorkspaceCost {\n    /// Workspace identifier\n    pub workspace_id: String,\n    /// Workspace name\n    pub workspace_name: String,\n    /// Path to workspace\n    pub workspace_path: PathBuf,\n    /// Whether the workspace has cost data\n    pub has_cost_data: bool,\n    /// Today's cost for this workspace\n    pub today_cost: Option<DailyCost>,\n    /// Total cost for this workspace\n    pub total_cost: f64,\n    /// Worker count for this workspace\n    pub worker_count: usize,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated cost information across all workspaces.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MultiWorkspaceCostSummary {\n    /// Cost information per workspace\n    pub by_workspace: HashMap<String, WorkspaceCost>,\n    /// Total cost across all workspaces\n    pub total_cost: f64,\n    /// Today's total cost across all workspaces\n    pub today_total: f64,\n    /// Total worker count across all workspaces\n    pub total_workers: usize,\n    /// Workspaces with cost data\n    pub workspaces_with_costs: usize,\n    /// Workspaces without cost data\n    pub workspaces_without_costs: usize,\n}\n\nimpl MultiWorkspaceCostSummary {\n    /// Get the cost for a specific workspace.\n    pub fn workspace_cost(&self, workspace_id: &str) -> Option<&WorkspaceCost> {\n        self.by_workspace.get(workspace_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the most expensive workspace.\n    pub fn most_expensive_workspace(&self) -> Option<&WorkspaceCost> {\n        self.by_workspace\n            .values()\n            .filter(|ws| ws.has_cost_data)\n            .max_by(|a, b| a.total_cost.partial_cmp(&b.total_cost).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the workspace with the most workers.\n    pub fn most_active_workspace(&self) -> Option<&WorkspaceCost> {\n        self.by_workspace\n            .values()\n            .max_by_key(|ws| ws.worker_count)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Aggregator for costs across multiple workspaces.\npub struct MultiWorkspaceCostAggregator {\n    /// Path to each workspace's cost database\n    workspace_dbs: HashMap<String, PathBuf>,\n}\n\nimpl MultiWorkspaceCostAggregator {\n    /// Create a new aggregator from a map of workspace_id -> database_path.\n    pub fn new(workspace_dbs: HashMap<String, PathBuf>) -> Self {\n        Self { workspace_dbs }\n    }\n\n    /// Create an aggregator from workspace paths.\n    ///\n    /// Each workspace should have a `.forge/costs.db` file.\n    pub fn from_workspace_paths(\n        workspaces: HashMap<String, (String, PathBuf)>,\n    ) -> Result<Self> {\n        let mut workspace_dbs = HashMap::new();\n\n        for (id, (_name, path)) in workspaces {\n            let cost_db_path = path.join(\".forge\").join(\"costs.db\");\n            if cost_db_path.exists() {\n                debug!(\"Found cost DB for workspace {}: {:?}\", id, cost_db_path);\n                workspace_dbs.insert(id, cost_db_path);\n            } else {\n                debug!(\"No cost DB found for workspace {} at {:?}\", id, cost_db_path);\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(Self { workspace_dbs })\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of workspaces with cost databases.\n    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {\n        self.workspace_dbs.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are any workspaces.\n    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n        self.workspace_dbs.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Aggregate today's costs across all workspaces.\n    pub fn aggregate_today_costs(&self) -> Result<MultiWorkspaceCostSummary> {\n        let today = Utc::now().date_naive();\n        self.aggregate_costs_for_date(today)\n    }\n\n    /// Aggregate costs for a specific date across all workspaces.\n    pub fn aggregate_costs_for_date(&self, date: NaiveDate) -> Result<MultiWorkspaceCostSummary> {\n        let mut summary = MultiWorkspaceCostSummary::default();\n\n        for (workspace_id, db_path) in &self.workspace_dbs {\n            let workspace_name = workspace_id.clone(); // In real usage, this would come from config\n\n            match self.query_workspace_cost(db_path, date) {\n                Ok(Some(daily_cost)) => {\n                    let cost = daily_cost.total_cost_usd;\n                    let ws_cost = WorkspaceCost {\n                        workspace_id: workspace_id.clone(),\n                        workspace_name: workspace_name.clone(),\n                        workspace_path: db_path\n                            .parent()\n                            .and_then(|p| p.parent())\n                            .unwrap_or(db_path)\n                            .to_path_buf(),\n                        has_cost_data: true,\n                        today_cost: Some(daily_cost),\n                        total_cost: cost,\n                        worker_count: 0, // Would be populated separately\n                    };\n\n                    summary.total_cost += cost;\n                    summary.today_total += cost;\n                    summary.workspaces_with_costs += 1;\n                    summary.by_workspace.insert(workspace_id.clone(), ws_cost);\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    // Workspace has no cost data for this date\n                    summary.workspaces_without_costs += 1;\n                    summary.by_workspace.insert(\n                        workspace_id.clone(),\n                        WorkspaceCost {\n                            workspace_id: workspace_id.clone(),\n                            workspace_name,\n                            workspace_path: db_path\n                                .parent()\n                                .and_then(|p| p.parent())\n                                .unwrap_or(db_path)\n                                .to_path_buf(),\n                            has_cost_data: false,\n                            today_cost: None,\n                            total_cost: 0.0,\n                            worker_count: 0,\n                        },\n                    );\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to query cost for workspace {}: {}\", workspace_id, e);\n                    summary.workspaces_without_costs += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        info!(\n            \"Aggregated costs for {} workspaces: ${:.2} total\",\n            summary.workspaces_with_costs,\n            summary.total_cost\n        );\n\n        Ok(summary)\n    }\n\n    /// Aggregate costs across all workspaces for a date range.\n    pub fn aggregate_costs_range(\n        &self,\n        start_date: NaiveDate,\n        end_date: NaiveDate,\n    ) -> Result<MultiWorkspaceCostSummary> {\n        let mut summary = MultiWorkspaceCostSummary::default();\n\n        for (workspace_id, db_path) in &self.workspace_dbs {\n            let workspace_name = workspace_id.clone();\n\n            match self.query_workspace_cost_range(db_path, start_date, end_date) {\n                Ok(cost) => {\n                    let ws_cost = WorkspaceCost {\n                        workspace_id: workspace_id.clone(),\n                        workspace_name,\n                        workspace_path: db_path\n                            .parent()\n                            .and_then(|p| p.parent())\n                            .unwrap_or(db_path)\n                            .to_path_buf(),\n                        has_cost_data: true,\n                        today_cost: None,\n                        total_cost: cost,\n                        worker_count: 0,\n                    };\n\n                    summary.total_cost += cost;\n                    summary.workspaces_with_costs += 1;\n                    summary.by_workspace.insert(workspace_id.clone(), ws_cost);\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to query cost range for workspace {}: {}\", workspace_id, e);\n                    summary.workspaces_without_costs += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(summary)\n    }\n\n    /// Query cost for a specific workspace on a specific date.\n    fn query_workspace_cost(\n        &self,\n        db_path: &Path,\n        date: NaiveDate,\n    ) -> Result<Option<DailyCost>> {\n        let db = CostDatabase::open(db_path)?;\n        let query = crate::query::CostQuery::new(&db);\n\n        match query.get_costs_for_date(date) {\n            Ok(daily) => {\n                if daily.call_count > 0 {\n                    Ok(Some(daily))\n                } else {\n                    Ok(None)\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Treat query errors as no data rather than hard failures\n                warn!(\"Cost query failed for {:?}: {}\", db_path, e);\n                Ok(None)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Query cost for a specific workspace over a date range.\n    fn query_workspace_cost_range(\n        &self,\n        db_path: &Path,\n        start_date: NaiveDate,\n        end_date: NaiveDate,\n    ) -> Result<f64> {\n        let db = CostDatabase::open(db_path)?;\n        let conn = db.connection();\n        let conn = conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            CostError::Query(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let start = start_date.format(\"%Y-%m-%d\").to_string();\n        let end = end_date.format(\"%Y-%m-%d\").to_string();\n\n        let total: f64 = conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT COALESCE(SUM(cost_usd), 0)\n                 FROM api_calls\n                 WHERE DATE(timestamp) BETWEEN ?1 AND ?2\",\n                rusqlite::params![start, end],\n                |row| row.get(0),\n            )\n            .unwrap_or(0.0);\n\n        Ok(total)\n    }\n\n    /// Get cost breakdown by model across all workspaces.\n    pub fn aggregate_model_costs(\n        &self,\n        start_date: Option<NaiveDate>,\n        end_date: Option<NaiveDate>,\n    ) -> Result<HashMap<String, f64>> {\n        let mut model_costs: HashMap<String, f64> = HashMap::new();\n\n        for db_path in self.workspace_dbs.values() {\n            let db = CostDatabase::open(db_path)?;\n            let query = crate::query::CostQuery::new(&db);\n\n            match query.get_model_costs(start_date, end_date) {\n                Ok(costs) => {\n                    for model_cost in costs {\n                        *model_costs.entry(model_cost.model).or_insert(0.0) += model_cost.total_cost_usd;\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to get model costs for {:?}: {}\", db_path, e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(model_costs)\n    }\n\n    /// Get cost breakdown by workspace for today.\n    pub fn get_workspace_breakdown_today(&self) -> Result<Vec<(String, f64)>> {\n        let mut breakdown = Vec::new();\n\n        for (workspace_id, db_path) in &self.workspace_dbs {\n            let db = CostDatabase::open(db_path)?;\n            let query = crate::query::CostQuery::new(&db);\n\n            match query.get_today_costs() {\n                Ok(daily) => {\n                    breakdown.push((workspace_id.clone(), daily.total_cost_usd));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to get today's costs for {}: {}\", workspace_id, e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        breakdown.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n\n        Ok(breakdown)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Helper function to create a workspace cost database path.\npub fn workspace_cost_db_path(workspace_path: &Path) -> PathBuf {\n    workspace_path.join(\".forge\").join(\"costs.db\")\n}\n\n/// Check if a workspace has cost data.\npub fn workspace_has_cost_data(workspace_path: &Path) -> bool {\n    workspace_cost_db_path(workspace_path).exists()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_cost_db_path() {\n        let path = Path::new(\"/home/coding/FORGE\");\n        let db_path = workspace_cost_db_path(path);\n        assert_eq!(db_path, PathBuf::from(\"/home/coding/FORGE/.forge/costs.db\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_has_cost_data() {\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let workspace_path = temp_dir.path();\n\n        // No cost data initially\n        assert!(!workspace_has_cost_data(workspace_path));\n\n        // Create .forge directory\n        let forge_dir = workspace_path.join(\".forge\");\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&forge_dir).unwrap();\n\n        // Still no cost data\n        assert!(!workspace_has_cost_data(workspace_path));\n\n        // Create cost DB file\n        let db_path = workspace_cost_db_path(workspace_path);\n        std::fs::write(&db_path, b\"\").unwrap();\n\n        // Now has cost data\n        assert!(workspace_has_cost_data(workspace_path));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_multi_workspace_cost_aggregator_new() {\n        let mut dbs = HashMap::new();\n        dbs.insert(\"forge\".to_string(), PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/forge/.forge/costs.db\"));\n        dbs.insert(\"other\".to_string(), PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/other/.forge/costs.db\"));\n\n        let aggregator = MultiWorkspaceCostAggregator::new(dbs);\n        assert_eq!(aggregator.len(), 2);\n        assert!(!aggregator.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_multi_workspace_cost_aggregator_empty() {\n        let aggregator = MultiWorkspaceCostAggregator::new(HashMap::new());\n        assert_eq!(aggregator.len(), 0);\n        assert!(aggregator.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_multi_workspace_cost_summary_default() {\n        let summary = MultiWorkspaceCostSummary::default();\n        assert_eq!(summary.total_cost, 0.0);\n        assert_eq!(summary.today_total, assert!(summary.most_expensive_workspace().is_none());\n        assert!(summary.most_active_workspace().is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_cost_structure() {\n        let ws_cost = WorkspaceCost {\n            workspace_id: \"test\".to_string(),\n            workspace_name: \"Test Workspace\".to_string(),\n            workspace_path: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n assert_eq!(ws_cost.total_cost, 10.50);\n        assert_eq!(ws_cost.worker_count, 3);\n        assert!(ws_cost.has_cost_data);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_multi_workspace_cost_summary_most_expensive() {\n        let mut summary = MultiWorkspaceCostSummary::default();\n        summary.total_cost = 30.0;\n\n        let mut by_workspace = HashMap::new();\n\n        by_workspace.insert(\n            \"cheap\".to_string(),\n            WorkspaceCost {\n                workspace_id: \"cheap\".to_string(),\n                workspace_name:           worker_count: 1,\n            },\n        );\n\n        by_workspace.insert(\n            \"expensive\".to_string(),\n            WorkspaceCost {\n                workspace_id: std::result::Result<Self::Value, A::Error>\n127\t            where\n128\t                A: serde::de::SeqAccess<'de>,\n129\t            {\n130\t                let mut registry =       registry.workspaces.push(workspace);\n135\t                    registry.index.insert(id, registry.workspaces.len() - 1);\n136\t                }\n137\t\n138\t                Ok(registry)\n139\t  /// Add a workspace to the registry.\n156\t    pub fn add(&mut self, workspace: WorkspaceConfig) {\n157\t        let id = workspace.id.clone();\n158\t        self.workspaces.push(workspace);\n159\t        self.index.insert(id, self.workspaces.len() - 1);\n160\t    }\n161\t\n162\t    /// Remove a workspace by ID.\n163\t    pub fn remove(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<WorkspaceConfig> {\n164\t self.workspaces.remove(idx);\n168\t            // Then rebuild index with correct indices\n169\t            self.index.clear();\n170\t            for (i, ws) in self.workspaces.iter().enumerate() {\n171\t                self.index.insert(ws.id.clone(), i);\n172\t            }\n173\t            Some(workspace)\n174\t        } else {\n175\t            None\n176\t        }\n177\t }\n183\t\n184\t    /// Get a mutable reference to a workspace by ID.\n185\t    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut WorkspaceConfig> {\n186\t        if let Some(&idx) = self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n212\t        if !self.index.contains_key(id) {\n213\t            return Err(ForgeError::WorkerNotFound {\n214\t                worker_id: format!(\"workspace {}\", id),\n215\t            });\n216\t        }\n217\t\n218\t        // Reorder by setting the current workspace to priority 0\n219\t        // and shifting others down\n220\t        if let Some(ws) = return Ok(Self::new());\n276\t        }\n277\t\n278\t        let content = fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n279\t            operation: \"read\".to_string(),\n280\t            path: path.to_path_buf(),\n281\t            source: e,\n282\t        })?;\n283\t\n284\t        let mut registry: Self = serde_yaml::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| {\n285\t self.index.clear();\n326\t        for (i, ws) in self.workspaces.iter().enumerate() {\n327\t            self.index.insert(ws.id.clone(), i);\n328\t        }\n329\t    }\n330\t\n331\t    /// Validate ============================================================\n418\t// Cross-Workspace Bead Visibility\n419\t// ============================================================\n420\t\n421\t/// Bead information from a workspace.\n422\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n423\tpub struct WorkspaceBead {\n424\t    /// Bead ID\n425\t    pub id: String,\n426\t    /// Workspace this bead {\n443\t    /// All beads across workspaces\n444\t    pub beads: Vec<WorkspaceBead>,\n445\t    /// Beads grouped by workspace\n446\t    pub by_workspace: HashMap<String, Vec<WorkspaceBead>>,\n447\t    /// Total open beads\n448\t    pub total_open: usize,\n449\t    /// Total closed beads\n450\t    pub total_closed: usize,\n451\t    /// Unassigned beads\n452\t    pub unassigned: usize,\n453\t}\n454\t\n455\timpl CrossWorkspaceBeadResult {\n456\t    /// Get beads for a specific workspace.\n457\t    pub fn beads_for_workspace(&self, workspace_id: &str) -> Vec<&WorkspaceBead> {\n458\t        self.by_workspace\n459\t            .get(workspace_id)\n460\t            .map(|v| v.iter().collect())\n461\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n462\t    }\n463\t\n464\t    /// Get open self.beads\n475\t            .iter()\n476\t            .filter(|b| b.assignee.is_none() || b.assignee.as_deref() == Some(\"none\"))\n477\t            .collect()\n478\t    }\n479\t}\n480\t\n481\t/// Query beads across multiple workspaces.\n482\t///\n483\t/// This function reads beads JSONL files from each workspace's .beads directory\n484\t/// and aggregates them into a unified view.\n485\t///\n486\t/// # Example\n487\t///\n488\t/// ```no_run\n489\t/// use forge_core::{WorkspaceRegistry, query_beads_cross_workspace};\n490\t///\n491\t/// fn main() -> forge_core::Result<()> {\n492\t///     let registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n493\t///     let result = query_beads_cross_workspace(&registry)?;\n494\t///\n495\t///     println!(\"Found {} beads across {} workspaces\",\n496\t///         result.beads.len(),\n497\t///         result.by_workspace.len()\n498\t///     );\n499\t///\n500\t///     Ok(())\n501\t/// }\n502\t/// ```\n503\tpub fn query_beads_cross_workspace(\n504\t    registry: &WorkspaceRegistry,\n505\t) -> Result<CrossWorkspaceBeadResult> {\n506\t    let mut result = CrossWorkspaceBeadResult::default();\n507\t\n508\t    for ws in registry.all() {\n509\t        let beads_dir = ws.beads_dir();\n510\t\n511\t        if !beads_dir.exists() {\n512\t            continue;\n513\t        }\n514\t\n515\t        let issues_path = beads_dir.join(\"issues.jsonl\");\n516\t\n517\t        if !issues_path.exists() {\n518\t            continue;\n519\t        }\n520\t\n521\t        // Read and parse beads JSONL\n522\t        match read_beads_from_jsonl(&issues_path, &ws.id, &ws.name) {\n523\t            Ok(mut beads) => {\n524\t                result.total_open += beads.iter().filter(|b| b.status == \"open\" || b.status == \"in-progress\").count();\n525\t                result.total_closed += beads.iter().filter(|b| b.status == \"closed\").count();\n526\t                result.unassigned += beads.iter().filter(|b| b.assignee.is_none() || b.assignee.as_deref() == Some(\"none\")).count();\n527\t\n528\t                result.by_workspace.insert(ws.id.clone(), beads.clone());\n529\t                result.beads.append(&mut beads);\n530\t            }\n531\t            Err(e) => {\n532\t                tracing::warn!(\n533\t                    \"Failed to read beads from workspace {}: {}\",\n534\t                    ws.id,\n535\t                    e\n536\t                );\n537\t            }\n538\t        }\n539\t    }\n540\t\n541\t    tracing::debug!(\n542\t        \"Queried {} beads across {} workspaces ({} open, {} closed)\",\n543\t        result.beads.len(),\n544\t        result.by_workspace.len(),\n545\t        result.total_open,\n546\t        result.total_closed\n547\t    );\n548\t\n549\t    Ok(result)\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t/// Read beads from a JSONL file.\n553\tfn read_beads_from_jsonl(\n554\t    path: &Path,\n555\t    workspace_id: &str,\n556\t    workspace_name: &str,\n557\t) -> Result<Vec<WorkspaceBead>> {\n558\t    let content = fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n559\t        operation: \"read\".to_string(),\n560\t        path: path.to_path_buf(),\n561\t        source: e,\n562\t    })?;\n563\t\n564\t    let mut beads = Vec::new();\n565\t\n566\t    for line in content.lines() {\n567\t        if line.trim().is_empty() {\n568\t            continue;\n569\t        }\n570\t\n571\t        // Parse JSONL line\n572\t        if let Ok(value) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line) {\n573\t            let id = value.get(\"id\")\n574\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n575\t                .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n576\t                .to_string();\n577\t\n578\t            let title = value.get(\"title\")\n579\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n580\t                .map(|s| s.to_string());\n581\t\n582\t            let status = value.get(\"status\")\n583\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n584\t                .unwrap_or(\"open\")\n585\t                .to_string();\n586\t\n587\t            let assignee = value.get(\"assignee\")\n588\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n589\t                .filter(|s| !s.is_empty() && *s != \"none\")\n590\t                .map(|s| s.to_string());\n591\t\n592\t            let priority = value.get(\"priority\")\n593\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n594\t                .map(|s| s.to_string());\n595\t\n596\t            beads.push(WorkspaceBead {\n597\t                id,\n598\t                workspace_id: workspace_id.to_string(),\n599\t                workspace_name: workspace_name.to_string(),\n600\t                title,\n601\t                status,\n602\t                assignee,\n603\t                priority,\n604\t            });\n605\t        }\n606\t    }\n607\t\n608\t    Ok(beads)\n609\t}\n610\t\n611\t/// Get bead count for a specific Multi-workspace coordination for FORGE.\n//!\n//! This module provides the workspace registry and management for coordinating\n//! multiple FORGE workspaces from a single dashboard.\n\nuse crate::{ForgeError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Deserializer, Serialize, Serializer};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::fs;\nuse std::fmt;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn};\n\n/// Configuration for a monitored workspace.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WorkspaceConfig {\n    /// Unique identifier for this workspace\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Human-readable name\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Absolute path to the workspace root\n    pub path: PathBuf,\n    /// Whether this workspace is actively monitored\n    #[serde(default = \"default_enabled\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Priority for display ordering (lower = higher priority)\n    #[serde(default = \"default_priority\")]\n    pub priority: u32,\n    /// Optional description\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub description: Option<String>,\n}\n\nfn default_enabled() -> bool { true }\nfn default_priority() -> u32 { 100 }\n\nimpl WorkspaceConfig {\n    /// Create a new workspace configuration.\n    pub fn new(id: impl Into<String>, name: impl Into<String>, path: impl Into<PathBuf>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: id.into(),\n            name: name.into(),\n            path: path.into(),\n            enabled: true,\n            priority: 100,\n            description: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the workspace path exists and is accessible.\n    pub fn is_accessible(&self) -> bool {\n        self.path.exists() && self.path.is_dir()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the path to the status directory for this workspace.\n    pub fn status_dir(&self) -> PathBuf {\n        self.path.join(\".forge\").join(\"status\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get the path to the logs directory for this workspace.\n    pub fn logs_dir(&self) -> PathBuf {\n        self.path.join(\".forge\").join(\"logs\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get the path to the cost database for this workspace.\n    pub fn cost_db_path(&self) -> PathBuf {\n        self.path.join(\".forge\").join(\"costs.db\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get the path to the beads directory for this workspace.\n    pub fn beads_dir(&self) -> PathBuf {\n        self.path.join(\".beads\")\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this workspace has a cost database.\n    pub fn has_cost_data(&self) -> bool {\n        self.cost_db_path().exists()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this workspace has beads.\n    pub fn has_beads(&self) -> bool {\n        self.beads_dir().exists()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Registry of monitored workspaces.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct WorkspaceRegistry {\n    /// All configured workspaces\n    workspaces: Vec<WorkspaceConfig>,\n    /// Index by workspace ID for fast lookup\n    index: HashMap<String, usize>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for WorkspaceRegistry {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n// Custom serialization: only serialize workspaces, skip index (it's rebuilt)\nimpl Serialize for WorkspaceRegistry {\n    fn serialize<S>(&self, serializer: S) -> std::result::Result<S::Ok, S::Error>\n    where\n        S: Serializer,\n    {\n        self.workspaces.serialize(serializer)\n    }\n}\n\n// Custom deserialization: deserialize workspaces and rebuild index\nimpl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for WorkspaceRegistry {\n    fn deserialize<D>(deserializer: D) -> std::result::Result<Self, D::Error>\n    where\n        D: Deserializer<'de>,\n    {\n        struct WorkspaceRegistryVisitor;\n\n        impl<'de> serde::de::Visitor<'de> for WorkspaceRegistryVisitor {\n            type Value = WorkspaceRegistry;\n\n            fn expecting(&self, formatter: &mut fmt::Formatter) -> fmt::Result {\n                formatter.write_str(\"a sequence of workspace configurations\")\n            }\n\n            fn visit_seq<A>(self, mut seq: A) -> std::result::Result<Self::Value, A::Error>\n            where\n                A: serde::de::SeqAccess<'de>,\n            {\n                let mut registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n\n                while let Some(workspace) = seq.next_element::<WorkspaceConfig>()? {\n                    let id = workspace.id.clone();\n                    registry.workspaces.push(workspace);\n                    registry.index.insert(id, registry.workspaces.len() - 1);\n                }\n\n                Ok(registry)\n            }\n        }\n\n        deserializer.deserialize_seq(WorkspaceRegistryVisitor)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl WorkspaceRegistry {\n    /// Create an empty workspace registry.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            workspaces: Vec::new(),\n            index: HashMap::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a workspace to the registry.\n    pub fn add(&mut self, workspace: WorkspaceConfig) {\n        let id = workspace.id.clone();\n        self.workspaces.push(workspace);\n        self.index.insert(id, self.workspaces.len() - 1);\n    }\n\n    /// Remove a workspace by ID.\n    pub fn remove(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<WorkspaceConfig> {\n        if let Some(&idx) = self.index.get(id) {\n            self.index.remove(id);\n            // Remove from vector first (this shifts subsequent elements)\n            let workspace = self.workspaces.remove(idx);\n            // Then rebuild index with correct indices\n            self.index.clear();\n            for (i, ws) in self.workspaces.iter().enumerate() {\n                self.index.insert(ws.id.clone(), i);\n            }\n            Some(workspace)\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a workspace by ID.\n    pub fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&WorkspaceConfig> {\n        self.index.get(id).and_then(|&idx| self.workspaces.get(idx))\n    }\n\n    /// Get a mutable reference to a workspace by ID.\n    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut WorkspaceConfig> {\n        if let Some(&idx) = self.index.get(id) {\n            self.workspaces.get_mut(idx)\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get all workspaces sorted by priority.\n    pub fn all(&self) -> Vec<&WorkspaceConfig> {\n        let mut workspaces: Vec<_> = self.workspaces.iter().collect();\n        workspaces.sort_by_key(|ws| ws.priority);\n        workspaces\n    }\n\n    /// Get only enabled workspaces.\n    pub fn enabled(&self) -> Vec<&WorkspaceConfig> {\n        self.all().into_iter().filter(|ws| ws.enabled).collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current/active workspace (first enabled by priority).\n    pub fn current(&self) -> Option<&WorkspaceConfig> {\n        self.enabled().first().copied()\n    }\n\n    /// Set the current workspace by moving it to highest priority.\n    pub fn set_current(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        if !self.index.contains_key(id) {\n            return Err(ForgeError::WorkerNotFound {\n                worker_id: format!(\"workspace {}\", id),\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Reorder by setting the current workspace to priority 0\n        // and shifting others down\n        if let Some(ws) = self.get_mut(id) {\n            let old_priority = ws.priority;\n            ws.priority = 0;\n\n            // Shift all other workspaces down\n            for other_ws in &mut self.workspaces {\n                if other_ws.id != id && other_ws.priority < old_priority {\n                    other_ws.priority += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get workspace count.\n    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {\n        self.workspaces.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if registry is empty.\n    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n        self.workspaces.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Discover workspaces from a config file or directory.\n    pub fn discover_from_path(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {\n        let mut registry = Self::new();\n\n        // If path is a workspace directory, add it\n        if path.exists() && path.is_dir() {\n            let forge_dir = path.join(\".forge\");\n            if forge_dir.exists() {\n                let id = path.file_name()\n                    .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"default\")\n                    .to_string();\n\n                let ws = WorkspaceConfig::new(\n                    id.clone(),\n                    id.clone(),\n                    path\n                );\n                registry.add(ws);\n                info!(\"Discovered workspace: {} at {:?}\", id, path);\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(registry)\n    }\n\n    /// Load workspace registry from a YAML configuration file.\n    pub fn load_from_file(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {\n        if !path.exists() {\n            warn!(\"Workspace config file not found: {:?}\", path);\n            return Ok(Self::new());\n        }\n\n        let content = fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n            operation: \"read\".to_string(),\n            path: path.to_path_buf(),\n            source: e,\n        })?;\n\n        let mut registry: Self = serde_yaml::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::YamlParse {\n                context: format!(\"workspace config at {:?}\", path),\n                message: e.to_string(),\n            }\n        })?;\n\n        // Rebuild index\n        registry.rebuild_index();\n\n        info!(\"Loaded {} workspaces from {:?}\", registry.len(), path);\n        Ok(registry)\n    }\n\n    /// Save workspace registry to a YAML configuration file.\n    pub fn save_to_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n            fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n                operation: \"create directory\".to_string(),\n                path: parent.to_path_buf(),\n                source: e,\n            })?;\n        }\n\n        let yaml = serde_yaml::to_string(self).map_err(|e| ForgeError::YamlParse {\n            context: \"workspace registry serialization\".to_string(),\n            message: e.to_string(),\n        })?;\n\n        fs::write(path, yaml).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n            operation: \"write\".to_string(),\n            path: path.to_path_buf(),\n            source: e,\n        })?;\n\n        debug!(\"Saved {} workspaces to {:?}\", self.len(), path);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Rebuild the index after deserialization or manual modification.\n    fn rebuild_index(&mut self) {\n        self.index.clear();\n        for (i, ws) in self.workspaces.iter().enumerate() {\n            self.index.insert(ws.id.clone(), i);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Validate all workspaces and report issues.\n    pub fn validate(&self) -> Vec<WorkspaceIssue> {\n        let mut issues = Vec::new();\n\n        for ws in &self.workspaces {\n            if !ws.enabled {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            if !ws.is_accessible() {\n                issues.push(WorkspaceIssue {\n                    workspace_id: ws.id.clone(),\n                    severity: IssueSeverity::Error,\n                    message: format!(\"Workspace path does not exist: {:?}\", ws.path),\n                });\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check for required directories\n            if !ws.status_dir().exists() {\n                issues.push(WorkspaceIssue {\n                    workspace_id: ws.id.clone(),\n                    severity: IssueSeverity::Warning,\n                    message: format!(\"Status directory not found: {:?}\", ws.status_dir()),\n                });\n            }\n\n            if !ws.logs_dir().exists() {\n                issues.push(WorkspaceIssue {\n                    workspace_id: ws.id.clone(),\n                    severity: IssueSeverity::Warning,\n                    message: format!(\"Logs directory not found: {:?}\", ws.logs_dir()),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        issues\n    }\n\n    /// Get aggregated statistics across all workspaces.\n    pub fn aggregate_stats(&self) -> WorkspaceAggregateStats {\n        let mut stats = WorkspaceAggregateStats::default();\n\n        for ws in &self.workspaces {\n            if !ws.enabled || !ws.is_accessible() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            stats.total_workspaces += 1;\n\n            if ws.has_cost_data() {\n                stats.workspaces_with_costs += 1;\n            }\n\n            if ws.has_beads() {\n                stats.workspaces_with_beads += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        stats\n    }\n}\n\n/// Issues found during workspace validation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct WorkspaceIssue {\n    pub workspace_id: String,\n    pub severity: IssueSeverity,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Severity of workspace issues.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum IssueSeverity {\n    Warning,\n    Error,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated statistics across workspaces.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WorkspaceAggregateStats {\n    pub total_workspaces: usize,\n    pub workspaces_with_costs: usize,\n    pub workspaces_with_beads: usize,\n}\n\n// ============================================================\n// Cross-Workspace Bead Visibility\n// ============================================================\n\n/// Bead information from a workspace.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WorkspaceBead {\n    /// Bead ID\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Workspace this bead belongs to\n    pub workspace_id: String,\n    /// Workspace name\n    pub workspace_name: String,\n    /// Bead title (extracted from JSONL)\n    pub title: Option<String>,\n    /// Bead status (open/closed)\n    pub status: String,\n    /// Assignee\n    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n    /// Priority (if available)\n    pub priority: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Result of cross-workspace bead query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CrossWorkspaceBeadResult {\n    /// All beads across workspaces\n    pub beads: Vec<WorkspaceBead>,\n    /// Beads grouped by workspace\n    pub by_workspace: HashMap<String, Vec<WorkspaceBead>>,\n    /// Total open beads\n    pub total_open: usize,\n    /// Total closed beads\n    pub total_closed: usize,\n    /// Unassigned beads\n    pub unassigned: usize,\n}\n\nimpl CrossWorkspaceBeadResult {\n    /// Get beads for a specific workspace.\n    pub fn beads_for_workspace(&self, workspace_id: &str) -> Vec<&WorkspaceBead> {\n        self.by_workspace\n            .get(workspace_id)\n            .map(|v| v.iter().collect())\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n    }\n\n    /// Get open beads across all workspaces.\n    pub fn open_beads(&self) -> Vec<&WorkspaceBead> {\n        self.beads\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|b| b.status == \"open\" || b.status == \"in-progress\")\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Get unassigned beads.\n    pub fn unassigned_beads(&self) -> Vec<&WorkspaceBead> {\n        self.beads\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|b| b.assignee.is_none() || b.assignee.as_deref() == Some(\"none\"))\n            .collect()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Query beads across multiple workspaces.\n///\n/// This function reads beads JSONL files from each workspace's .beads directory\n/// and aggregates them into a unified view.\n///\n/// # Example\n///\n/// ```no_run\n/// use forge_core::{WorkspaceRegistry, query_beads_cross_workspace};\n///\n/// fn main() -> forge_core::Result<()> {\n///     let registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n///     let result = query_beads_cross_workspace(&registry)?;\n///\n///     println!(\"Found {} beads across {} workspaces\",\n///         result.beads.len(),\n///         result.by_workspace.len()\n///     );\n///\n///     Ok(())\n/// }\n/// ```\npub fn query_beads_cross_workspace(\n    registry: &WorkspaceRegistry,\n) -> Result<CrossWorkspaceBeadResult> {\n    let mut result = CrossWorkspaceBeadResult::default();\n\n    for ws in registry.all() {\n        let beads_dir = ws.beads_dir();\n\n        if !beads_dir.exists() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let issues_path = beads_dir.join(\"issues.jsonl\");\n\n        if !issues_path.exists() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Read and parse beads JSONL\n        match read_beads_from_jsonl(&issues_path, &ws.id, &ws.name) {\n            Ok(mut beads) => {\n                result.total_open += beads.iter().filter(|b| b.status == \"open\" || b.status == \"in-progress\").count();\n                result.total_closed += beads.iter().filter(|b| b.status == \"closed\").count();\n                result.unassigned += beads.iter().filter(|b| b.assignee.is_none() || b.assignee.as_deref() == Some(\"none\")).count();\n\n                result.by_workspace.insert(ws.id.clone(), beads.clone());\n                result.beads.append(&mut beads);\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    \"Failed to read beads from workspace {}: {}\",\n                    ws.id,\n                    e\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    tracing::debug!(\n        \"Queried {} beads across {} workspaces ({} open, {} closed)\",\n        result.beads.len(),\n        result.by_workspace.len(),\n        result.total_open,\n        result.total_closed\n    );\n\n    Ok(result)\n}\n\n/// Read beads from a JSONL file.\nfn read_beads_from_jsonl(\n    path: &Path,\n    workspace_id: &str,\n    workspace_name: &str,\n) -> Result<Vec<WorkspaceBead>> {\n    let content = fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n        operation: \"read\".to_string(),\n        path: path.to_path_buf(),\n        source: e,\n    })?;\n\n    let mut beads = Vec::new();\n\n    for line in content.lines() {\n        if line.trim().is_empty() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Parse JSONL line\n        if let Ok(value) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line) {\n            let id = value.get(\"id\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n                .to_string();\n\n            let title = value.get(\"title\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n            let status = value.get(\"status\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"open\")\n                .to_string();\n\n            let assignee = value.get(\"assignee\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .filter(|s| !s.is_empty() && *s != \"none\")\n                .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n            let priority = value.get(\"priority\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n            beads.push(WorkspaceBead {\n                id,\n                workspace_id: workspace_id.to_string(),\n                workspace_name: workspace_name.to_string(),\n                title,\n                status,\n                assignee,\n                priority,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(beads)\n}\n\n/// Get bead count for a specific workspace.\npub fn get_workspace_bead_count(workspace_path: &Path) -> Result<usize> {\n    let beads_dir = workspace_path.join(\".beads\");\n    let issues_path = beads_dir.join(\"issues.jsonl\");\n\n    if !issues_path.exists() {\n        return Ok(0);\n    }\n\n    let content = fs::read_to_string(&issues_path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n        operation: \"read\".to_string(),\n        path: issues_path.clone(),\n        source: e,\n    })?;\n\n    let count = content.lines().filter(|l| !l.trim().is_empty()).count();\n\n    Ok(count)\n}\n\n/// Get bead counts for all workspaces in a registry.\npub fn get_workspace_bead_counts(\n    registry: &WorkspaceRegistry,\n) -> Result<HashMap<String, usize>> {\n    let mut counts = HashMap::new();\n\n    for ws in registry.all() {\n        if !ws.is_accessible() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        match get_workspace_bead_count(&ws.path) {\n            Ok(count) => {\n                counts.insert(ws.id.clone(), count);\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    \"Failed to get bead count for workspace {}: {}\",\n                    ws.id,\n                    e\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(counts)\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_config_new() {\n        let ws = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"test\", \"Test Workspace\", \"/tmp/test\");\n        assert_eq!(ws.id, \"test\");\n        assert_eq!(ws.name, \"Test Workspace\");\n        assert_eq!(ws.path, PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"));\n        assert!(ws.enabled);\n        assert_eq!(ws.priority, 100);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_registry_add_remove() {\n        let mut registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n\n        let ws1 = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"ws1\", \"Workspace 1\", \"/tmp/ws1\");\n        let ws2 = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"ws2\", \"Workspace 2\", \"/tmp/ws2\");\n\n        registry.add(ws1.clone());\n        registry.add(ws2.clone());\n\n        assert_eq!(registry.len(), 2);\n        assert!(registry.get(\"ws1\").is_some());\n        assert!(registry.get(\"ws2\").is_some());\n\n        let removed = registry.remove(\"ws1\");\n        assert!(removed.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(removed.unwrap().id, \"ws1\");\n        assert_eq!(registry.len(), 1);\n        assert!(registry.get(\"ws1\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_registry_priority_sorting() {\n        let mut registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n\n        let mut ws1 = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"ws1\", \"Workspace 1\", \"/tmp/ws1\");\n        ws1.priority = 10;\n\n        let mut ws2 = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"ws2\", \"Workspace 2\", \"/tmp/ws2\");\n        ws2.priority = 5;\n\n        let mut ws3 = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"ws3\", \"Workspace 3\", \"/tmp/ws3\");\n        ws3.priority = 15;\n\n        registry.add(ws1);\n        registry.add(ws2);\n        registry.add(ws3);\n\n        let all = registry.all();\n        assert_eq!(all[0].id, \"ws2\"); // Priority 5\n        assert_eq!(all[1].id, \"ws1\"); // Priority 10\n        assert_eq!(all[2].id, \"ws3\"); // Priority 15\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_registry_set_current() {\n        let mut registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n\n        let mut ws1 = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"ws1\", \"Workspace 1\", \"/tmp/ws1\");\n        ws1.priority = 0;\n\n        let mut ws2 = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"ws2\", \"Workspace 2\", \"/tmp/ws2\");\n        ws2.priority = 1;\n\n        registry.add(ws1);\n        registry.add(ws2);\n\n        // Initially ws1 is current (priority 0)\n        assert_eq!(registry.current().map(|w| w.id.as_str()), Some(\"ws1\"));\n\n        // Set ws2 as current\n        registry.set_current(\"ws2\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(registry.current().map(|w| w.id.as_str()), Some(\"ws2\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_registry_enabled_filter() {\n        let mut registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n\n        let mut ws1 = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"ws1\", \"Workspace 1\", \"/tmp/ws1\");\n        ws1.enabled = true;\n\n        let mut ws2 = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"ws2\", \"Workspace 2\", \"/tmp/ws2\");\n        ws2.enabled = false;\n\n        registry.add(ws1);\n        registry.add(ws2);\n\n        assert_eq!(registry.enabled().len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(registry.enabled()[0].id, \"ws1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_registry_serialize() {\n        let mut registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n\n        let ws = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"test\", \"Test\", \"/tmp/test\");\n        registry.add(ws);\n\n        let yaml = serde_yaml::to_string(&registry).unwrap();\n        let loaded: WorkspaceRegistry = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(loaded.len(), 1);\n        assert!(loaded.get(\"test\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_discovery() {\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let workspace_path = temp_dir.path();\n\n        // Create .forge directory to make it a valid workspace\n        let forge_dir = workspace_path.join(\".forge\");\n        fs::create_dir_all(&forge_dir).unwrap();\n\n        let registry = WorkspaceRegistry::discover_from_path(workspace_path).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(registry.len(), 1);\n        assert!(registry.get(\n            workspace_path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()).unwrap()\n        ).is_some());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_workspace_aggregate_stats() {\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let ws_path = temp_dir.path();\n\n        let mut registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n\n        // Create a workspace with cost data\n        let ws = WorkspaceConfig::new(\"test\", \"Test\", ws_path);\n        registry.add(ws);\n\n        let stats = registry.aggregate_stats();\n        // Workspace /// Update cost for a workspace.\n151\t    pub fn update_cost(&mut self, workspace_id: &str, cost: f64) {\n152\t        if let Some(ws) = self.workspaces.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == workspace_id) self.update_cost(workspace_id, ws_cost.total_cost);\n184\t            }\n185\t        }\n186\t    }\n187\t\n188\t    /// Update bead counts from a map of workspace_id -> bead_count.\n189\t    pub fn                Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text_dim),\n288\t            )\n289\t        )\n290\t        .block(\n291\t            Block::default()\n292\t                .borders(Borders::ALL)\n293\t                .border_type(BorderType::Rounded)\n294\t                .border_style(Style::default().fg(theme.colors.border_dim))\n295\t        )\n296\t \"Path\",\n312\t    ];\n313\t\n314\t    // Build table rows\n315\t    let rows: Vec<Row> = data.workspaces\n316\t        .iter()\n317\t        .enumerate()\n318\t        .map(|(i, ws)| {\n319\t            let is_selected = i == data.selected_index;\n320\t            let base_style = if is_selected {\n321\t                Style::default()\n322\t Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text_dim)\n326\t            } else {\n327\t                Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text)\n328\t            };\n329\t\n330\t            let status = if } else {\n337\t                Span::styled(\"●\", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.status_healthy))\n338\t            };\n339\t\n340\t            let workers = if ws.has_workers {\n341\t                format!(\"{}\", ws.worker_count)\n342\t            } else {\n343\t                \"-\".to_string()\n344\t            };\n345\t\n346\t            let cost = if ws.has_costs {\n347\t format!(\"{}\", ws.bead_count)\n354\t            } else {\n355\t                \"-\".to_string()\n356\t            };\n357\t\n358\t            let path = if ws.path.len() > 30 {\n359\t                format!(\"...{}\", &ws.path[ws.path.len() - 27..])\n360\t            } else {\n361\t                ws.path.clone()\n362\t            };\n363\t\n364\t            let cells = vec![\n365\t        Constraint::Length(15), // Name\n383\t        Constraint::Length(8),  // Workers\n384\t        Constraint::Length(8),  // Cost\n385\t        Constraint::Length(8),  // Beads\n386\t .borders(Borders::ALL)\n393\t                .border_type(BorderType::Rounded)\n394\t                .border_style(Style::default().fg(theme.colors.border_dim))\n395\t        )\n396\t            Style::default()\n407\t                .bg(theme.colors.focus_highlight)\n408\t                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)\n409\t        );\n410\t\n411\t    f.render_widget(table, area);\n412\t}\n413\t\n414\t/// Draw the workspace footer with key hints.\n415\tfn draw_workspace_footer(f: &mut Frame, area: Rect, theme: &Theme) {\n416\t    let hints = vec![\n417\t Span::styled(\"[-]\", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.hotkey).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)),\n424\t        Span::styled(\" Remove \", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text)),\n425\t data.workspaces.push(create_test_workspace(\"ws1\"));\n561\t        data.workspaces.push(create_test_workspace(\"ws2\"));\n562\t\n563\t        assert_eq!(data.selected_index, 0);\n564\t\n565\t data.select_prev();\n572\t        assert_eq!(data.selected_index, 1);\n573\t    }\n574\t\n575\t    fn create_test_workspace(id: &str) -> WorkspaceDisplayInfo {\n576\t        WorkspaceDisplayInfo {\n577\t            id: id.to_string(),\n578\t            name: id.to_string(),\n579\t            path: format!(\"/tmp/{}\", id),\n580\t            enabled: true,\n581\t            is_current:        has_beads: false,\n588\t            bead_count: 0,\n589\t            last_updated: String::new(),\n590\t        }\n591\t    }\n592\t}\n593\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"632a3d95-f4f7-4674-9e06-ec98d9c6aa36","uuid":"73a00866-4933-4de5-94a3-42a1553f4238","timestamp":"2026-05-04T12:58:22.669Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/FORGE/crates/forge-tui/src/workspace_panel.rs","content":"//! Workspace management panel for multi-workspace coordination.\n//!\n//! This module provides the UI for managing multiple FORGE workspaces,\n//! including switching between workspaces and viewing aggregated statistics.\n\nuse crate::theme::Theme;\nuse forge_core::WorkspaceRegistry;\nuse forge_worker::discovery::CrossWorkspaceDiscoveryResult;\nuse forge_cost::MultiWorkspaceCostSummary;\nuse ratatui::{\n    layout::{Alignment, Constraint, Direction, Layout, Rect},\n    style::{Modifier, Style},\n    text::{Line, Span, Text},\n    widgets::{Block, BorderType, Borders, Cell, Clear, Paragraph, Row, Table, Wrap},\n    Frame,\n};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Data for the workspace panel display.\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\npub struct WorkspacePanelData {\n    /// All configured workspaces\n    pub workspaces: Vec<WorkspaceDisplayInfo>,\n    /// Currently selected workspace index\n    pub selected_index: usize,\n    /// Aggregated statistics across all workspaces\n    pub aggregate_stats: WorkspaceAggregateDisplay,\n    /// Validation issues found\n    pub issues: Vec<WorkspaceIssueDisplay>,\n    /// Whether workspace switching is in progress\n    pub switching: bool,\n}\n\n/// Display information for a single workspace.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct WorkspaceDisplayInfo {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub name: String,\n    pub path: String,\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub is_current: bool,\n    pub is_accessible: bool,\n    pub has_workers: bool,\n    pub worker_count: usize,\n    pub has_costs: bool,\n    pub total_cost: f64,\n    pub has_beads: bool,\n    pub bead_count: usize,\n    pub last_updated: String,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated statistics display.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct WorkspaceAggregateDisplay {\n    pub total_workspaces: usize,\n    pub enabled_workspaces: usize,\n    pub total_workers: usize,\n    pub total_cost: f64,\n    pub total_beads: usize,\n}\n\n/// Display wrapper for workspace issues.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct WorkspaceIssueDisplay {\n    pub workspace_id: String,\n    pub severity: String,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\nimpl WorkspacePanelData {\n    /// Create empty workspace panel data.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Update from a workspace registry.\n    pub fn update_from_registry(&mut self, registry: &WorkspaceRegistry, current_id: Option<&str>) {\n        self.workspaces.clear();\n\n        for ws in registry.all() {\n            let is_current = current_id.map_or(false, |id| id == ws.id);\n\n            self.workspaces.push(WorkspaceDisplayInfo {\n                id: ws.id.clone(),\n                name: ws.name.clone(),\n                path: ws.path.display().to_string(),\n                enabled: ws.enabled,\n                is_current,\n                is_accessible: ws.is_accessible(),\n                has_workers: false, // Will be updated by discovery\n                worker_count: 0,\n                has_costs: ws.has_cost_data(),\n                total_cost: 0.0, // Will be updated by cost query\n                has_beads: ws.has_beads(),\n                bead_count: 0,   // Will be updated by bead query\n                last_updated: String::new(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Update aggregate stats\n        self.aggregate_stats.total_workspaces = registry.len();\n        self.aggregate_stats.enabled_workspaces = registry.enabled().len();\n    }\n\n    /// Get the currently selected workspace.\n    pub fn selected(&self) -> Option<&WorkspaceDisplayInfo> {\n        if self.selected_index < self.workspaces.len() {\n            self.workspaces.get(self.selected_index)\n        } else {\n            self.workspaces.first()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Select the next workspace.\n    pub fn select_next(&mut self) {\n        if self.workspaces.is_empty() {\n            return;\n        }\n        self.selected_index = (self.selected_index + 1) % self.workspaces.len();\n    }\n\n    /// Select the previous workspace.\n    pub fn select_prev(&mut self) {\n        if self.workspaces.is_empty() {\n            return;\n        }\n        if self.selected_index == 0 {\n            self.selected_index = self.workspaces.len() - 1;\n        } else {\n            self.selected_index -= 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Select a specific workspace by index.\n    pub fn select_index(&mut self, index: usize) {\n        if index < self.workspaces.len() {\n            self.selected_index = index;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update worker count for a workspace.\n    pub fn update_worker_count(&mut self, workspace_id: &str, count: usize) {\n        if let Some(ws) = self.workspaces.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == workspace_id) {\n            ws.worker_count = count;\n            ws.has_workers = count > 0;\n            self.aggregate_stats.total_workers += count;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update cost for a workspace.\n    pub fn update_cost(&mut self, workspace_id: &str, cost: f64) {\n        if let Some(ws) = self.workspaces.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == workspace_id) {\n            ws.total_cost = cost;\n            self.aggregate_stats.total_cost += cost;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update bead count for a workspace.\n    pub fn update_bead_count(&mut self, workspace_id: &str, count: usize) {\n        if let Some(ws) = self.workspaces.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == workspace_id) {\n            ws.bead_count = count;\n            self.aggregate_stats.total_beads += count;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update worker counts from cross-workspace discovery result.\n    pub fn update_from_discovery(&mut self, discovery: &CrossWorkspaceDiscoveryResult) {\n        // Reset worker counts\n        self.aggregate_stats.total_workers = 0;\n\n        for (workspace_id, workers) in &discovery.by_workspace {\n            self.update_worker_count(workspace_id, workers.len());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update costs from multi-workspace cost summary.\n    pub fn update_from_cost_summary(&mut self, summary: &MultiWorkspaceCostSummary) {\n        // Reset total cost\n        self.aggregate_stats.total_cost = 0.0;\n\n        for (workspace_id, ws_cost) in &summary.by_workspace {\n            if ws_cost.has_cost_data {\n                self.update_cost(workspace_id, ws_cost.total_cost);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update bead counts from a map of workspace_id -> bead_count.\n    pub fn update_from_bead_counts(&mut self, bead_counts: &HashMap<String, usize>) {\n        // Reset total beads\n        self.aggregate_stats.total_beads = 0;\n\n        for (workspace_id, count) in bead_counts {\n            self.update_bead_count(workspace_id, *count);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Draw the workspace management panel.\npub fn draw_workspace_panel(\n    f: &mut Frame,\n    area: Rect,\n    data: &WorkspacePanelData,\n    theme: &Theme,\n) {\n    // Split into header, main content, and footer\n    let chunks = Layout::default()\n        .direction(Direction::Vertical)\n        .margin(0)\n        .constraints([\n            Constraint::Length(3),  // Header with stats\n            Constraint::Min(10),    // Main workspace list\n            Constraint::Length(3),  // Footer with hints\n        ])\n        .split(area);\n\n    draw_workspace_header(f, chunks[0], data, theme);\n    draw_workspace_list(f, chunks[1], data, theme);\n    draw_workspace_footer(f, chunks[2], theme);\n}\n\n/// Draw the workspace header with aggregated statistics.\nfn draw_workspace_header(\n    f: &mut Frame,\n    area: Rect,\n    data: &WorkspacePanelData,\n    theme: &Theme,\n) {\n    let title = Span::styled(\n        \"Multi-Workspace Management\",\n        Style::default()\n            .fg(theme.colors.header)\n            .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n    );\n\n    let stats = vec![\n        Span::styled(\n            format!(\" Workspaces: {}/{}\", data.aggregate_stats.enabled_workspaces, data.aggregate_stats.total_workspaces),\n            Style::default().fg(theme.colors.hotkey),\n        ),\n        Span::raw(\" | \"),\n        Span::styled(\n            format!(\"Workers: {}\", data.aggregate_stats.total_workers),\n            Style::default().fg(theme.colors.status_healthy),\n        ),\n        Span::raw(\" | \"),\n        Span::styled(\n            format!(\"Cost: ${:.2}\", data.aggregate_stats.total_cost),\n            Style::default().fg(theme.colors.status_warning),\n        ),\n        Span::raw(\" | \"),\n        Span::styled(\n            format!(\"Beads: {}\", data.aggregate_stats.total_beads),\n            Style::default().fg(theme.colors.action_view),\n        ),\n    ];\n\n    let header_text = Text::from(vec![\n        Line::from(title),\n        Line::from(stats),\n    ]);\n\n    let paragraph = Paragraph::new(header_text)\n        .block(\n            Block::default()\n                .borders(Borders::ALL)\n                .border_type(BorderType::Rounded)\n                .border_style(Style::default().fg(theme.colors.border_dim))\n        )\n        .alignment(Alignment::Center)\n        .wrap(Wrap { trim: true });\n\n    f.render_widget(paragraph, area);\n}\n\n/// Draw the workspace list table.\nfn draw_workspace_list(\n    f: &mut Frame,\n    area: Rect,\n    data: &WorkspacePanelData,\n    theme: &Theme,\n) {\n    if data.workspaces.is_empty() {\n        let no_workspaces = Paragraph::new(\n            Text::styled(\n                \"No workspaces configured.\\n\\nAdd workspaces in ~/.forge/config.yaml under workspaces.workspace_paths\",\n                Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text_dim),\n            )\n        )\n        .block(\n            Block::default()\n                .borders(Borders::ALL)\n                .border_type(BorderType::Rounded)\n                .border_style(Style::default().fg(theme.colors.border_dim))\n        )\n        .alignment(Alignment::Center)\n        .wrap(Wrap { trim: true });\n\n        f.render_widget(no_workspaces, area);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    // Define table headers\n    let header = vec![\n        \"Status\",\n        \"ID\",\n        \"Name\",\n        \"Workers\",\n        \"Cost\",\n        \"Beads\",\n        \"Path\",\n    ];\n\n    // Build table rows\n    let rows: Vec<Row> = data.workspaces\n        .iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .map(|(i, ws)| {\n            let is_selected = i == data.selected_index;\n            let base_style = if is_selected {\n                Style::default()\n                    .fg(theme.colors.focus_highlight)\n                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)\n            } else if !ws.enabled {\n                Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text_dim)\n            } else {\n                Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text)\n            };\n\n            let status = if ws.is_current {\n                Span::styled(\"★\", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.status_healthy))\n            } else if !ws.enabled {\n                Span::styled(\"○\", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text_dim))\n            } else if !ws.is_accessible {\n                Span::styled(\"✕\", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.status_error))\n            } else {\n                Span::styled(\"●\", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.status_healthy))\n            };\n\n            let workers = if ws.has_workers {\n                format!(\"{}\", ws.worker_count)\n            } else {\n                \"-\".to_string()\n            };\n\n            let cost = if ws.has_costs {\n                format!(\"${:.2}\", ws.total_cost)\n            } else {\n                \"-\".to_string()\n            };\n\n            let beads = if ws.has_beads {\n                format!(\"{}\", ws.bead_count)\n            } else {\n                \"-\".to_string()\n            };\n\n            let path = if ws.path.len() > 30 {\n                format!(\"...{}\", &ws.path[ws.path.len() - 27..])\n            } else {\n                ws.path.clone()\n            };\n\n            let cells = vec![\n                Cell::from(status),\n                Cell::from(ws.id.as_str()),\n                Cell::from(ws.name.as_str()),\n                Cell::from(workers),\n                Cell::from(cost),\n                Cell::from(beads),\n                Cell::from(path),\n            ];\n\n            Row::new(cells).style(base_style)\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Calculate column widths\n    let widths = vec![\n        Constraint::Length(8),  // Status\n        Constraint::Length(12), // ID\n        Constraint::Length(15), // Name\n        Constraint::Length(8),  // Workers\n        Constraint::Length(8),  // Cost\n        Constraint::Length(8),  // Beads\n        Constraint::Min(30),    // Path\n    ];\n\n    let table = Table::new(rows, widths)\n        .block(\n            Block::default()\n                .borders(Borders::ALL)\n                .border_type(BorderType::Rounded)\n                .border_style(Style::default().fg(theme.colors.border_dim))\n        )\n        .header(\n            Row::new(header.iter().map(|h| Cell::from(*h)))\n                .style(\n                    Style::default()\n                        .fg(theme.colors.header)\n                        .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)\n                )\n        )\n        .column_spacing(1)\n        .row_highlight_style(\n            Style::default()\n                .bg(theme.colors.focus_highlight)\n                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)\n        );\n\n    f.render_widget(table, area);\n}\n\n/// Draw the workspace footer with key hints.\nfn draw_workspace_footer(f: &mut Frame, area: Rect, theme: &Theme) {\n    let hints = vec![\n        Span::styled(\"[Enter]\", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.hotkey).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)),\n        Span::styled(\" Switch \", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text)),\n        Span::styled(\"[↑/↓]\", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.hotkey).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)),\n Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text)),\n        Span::styled(\"[Esc]\", Style::default().fg(theme.colors.hotkey).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)),\n        Span::styled(\" Back \",          .border_type(BorderType::Rounded)\n                .border_style(Style::default().fg(theme.colors.border_dim))\n        )\n        .alignment(Alignment::Center);\n\n    f.render_widget(paragraph, area);\n}\n\n/// Draw workspace detail overlay.\npub fn draw_workspace_detail_overlay(\n    f: &mut Frame,\n    data: &WorkspacePanelData,\n    theme: &Theme,\n) {\n    let
